title: Easy | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Getting Started
description:
## Getting Started

This is the **EASY 250 Points Competition.**

Your task in **this** challenge is to understand what problem we are trying to solve and build a prototype structure to be used in the Medium and hard challenges. 

**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).

Note: You are only required to build a subset of the application in this challenge. The rest will be covered in Medium and Hard challenges.


## What do you need to do in this Easy challenge?

In this easy challenge, the requirements from you are as follows. You need to design and build the functionality:

**Client Signup:**

This is where a client will be able to sign up with the following details: Name, Email, and Password.

**Client Login:**

This is where a client will be able to login with the following details: Email, and Password.

**Navbar:**

The navbar navigation will have two options: 1) Profile, and 2) Create Learning Space** **

**Client profile:**

When the client clicks on their profile from the navigation, a screen showing all the details of them should render. The required profile details are: Name, Biography and Country of residence, interests (ReactJS tags) and a placeholder photo thumbnail.


## Recommended learning material

[React Authentication Tutorial ��� How to Set Up Auth with Firebase V9 and React Router V6](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/react-firebase-authentication-and-crud-operations/)

[User Registration With Firebase and React | CSS-Tricks](https://css-tricks.com/user-registration-authentication-firebase-react/)

[react-tagsinput - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-tagsinput)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:



   * Create a folder with ���easy��� as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to take screenshots of all the screens you built in this challenge and place it in a folder called ���screenshots��� within the ���easy��� folder
   * Zip that ���easy��� folder and submit to the challenge.

title: Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS
description:
Welcome to our Topcoder Skill Builder "**ReactJS**" Competition! 

We are excited to kick-off our plans to help everyone skill up, have fun, and earn more money through Topcoder Gigs and Competitions. Jump-in, build your skills and as always let us know what you think and where Topcoder can continue to improve and bring competitions that are meaningful to you.

[ReactJS](https://reactjs.org/) (also known as React.js or React) is a free and open-source front-end JavaScript library for building user interfaces based on UI components. 

In this ReactJS Skill Builder Competition you will learn in depth what is ReactJS ,and build an awesome co-learning application that can be used by you, and other people to learn something great together. The content of this skill builder will not just help you understand the ReactJS, but also have a working ReactJS application that would prove your expertise in this domain. You will also encounter a lot of learning material in this Skill builder.


## Topcoder Skill Builder Competition Structure



* Skill Builder Competition will have three (3) problems (Easy, Medium, and Hard) to solve. 
* **IMPORTANT:** This Skill Builder Competition is intended for people seeking gig opportunities After the competition we will be adding you to the TaaS Pool of ReactJS Developers, where you would be able to be assigned to available gigs.


## Problem Statement

Co-Learn is a platform for people who get a thrill from learning and love to share a knowledge. The purpose of Co-learn is to create an environment for learning for the sake of learning.

In this Skill builder series, we will build an awesome colearning application. Although most of the focus will be on the frontend, you will be required to do a little of backend integration for the functionality to work. 


## Final Submission Guidelines

**What to Submit**

**You are required to submit on the Host challenge (this challenge) and on the sub-challenges (Easy, Medium, and Hard).**

On this Challenge, please submit a .txt file using the ���Submit��� button before the submission phase ends. In this file include:



* Your Topcoder handle (The one displayed in the top right corner near the Profile picture)
* ALL THE SUBMISSIONS WITHOUT ABOVE INFORMATION WILL BE REJECTED AND WON���T BE PAID. IMPORTANT: Submit the above details before the Submission Phase ends.


## Judging Criteria



* Frontend requirement implementation 50%
* User Experience 15%
* Rendering Performance 10%
* Code quality and code size 10% (using libraries like [Store | Redux](https://redux.js.org/api/store) will count positive) 
* Ease of deployment 10%
* Backend implementation 5% 


## Instructions



* We have prepared three problems: Easy, Medium, and Hard, which are worth 250, 500, 1000 points respectively. Points are based on the difficulty of the problem.
* The competitions are related to each other.
* The links to the problems/competitions are provided below.
* Each problem has some detailed requirements to achieve. We will judge your submission based on the criteria mentioned above.
* If there is a tie, the submission time will be taken into account.

[Here is the leaderboard with individual problem scores](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fWykiymzRLVJ_gLefMnLi6G8wK04TK9-rb8w-jYicoo/edit?usp=sharing). (Updated at the end of the Skill builder)

title: Hard | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS  | Annotations
description:
This is the **HARD 1000 Points Competition**

By now you have built the base of the application. It's time to add more and complete the existing functionality \
 \
 \
**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).


## What do you need to do in this Hard challenge?

In the application so far, we have built screens for customer authentication, homepage, learning spaces, and profiles. Now we will build some more screens.

**Creating a Learning Space**



* An authenticated user should be able to create a new learning space from the Navbar menu title ***Create Space***
* A new space creation screen should be rendered.
* The screen should have a form with the following fields: Title, description, prerequisites (ReactJS tags), thumbnail.

**Creating a Post**



* Within a learning space, the user should be able to create a new post.
* The post will have a title and a body. The body should use a Markdown text-editor input. You can refer to the reference to understand more about markdown editors.
* When viewing a post, the post can be saved locally as a PDF

**Creating a Comment**



* Within a learning space Post, the user should be able to add a new comment.
* The comment will have a body. The body should use a Markdown text-editor input. You can refer to the reference to understand more about markdown editors.

**Upvotes/Downvotes**



* The users should be able to upvote/downvote a Post, or a comment.

**Annotation**



* Annotation refers to a note by way of explanation or comment added to a text or diagram.
* Within a learning space, inside a post, a user can highlight a text, on highlight it will display an icon to annotate that text. On clicking the icon, a pop up should appear with two items. The first being the highlighted text, and the second being a text field where user can enter their thoughts about the highlighted text.  
* The user should be able to see the text highlighted and their annotation on hovering on it after save. 


## Recommended learning material

[Markdown Editor for React.](https://uiwjs.github.io/react-md-editor/)

[Generating a PDF file from React Components - Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44989119/generating-a-pdf-file-from-react-components)

[Annotation Example.gif](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1AfNytnWfkRK5hr34LIXSMxCWU7AMUIc5/view?usp=share_link)

[Text Annotation And Highlighting Library - Annotate.js | CSS Script](https://www.cssscript.com/text-annotation-highlighting/)

[Medium-like text highlighting in React | by Pargles Dall'Oglio | JavaScript in Plain English](https://javascript.plainenglish.io/medium-like-text-highlighting-in-react-afa35a29a81a)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:

   * Create a folder with ***hard*** as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to screen record a video of all the functionality you built in this challenge. Upload the video on your google drive and share the link in a text file that you will place in the ***hard*** folder.
   * Zip that ***hard*** folder and submit to the challenge.

title: Medium | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Co-learning
description:
This is the **MEDIUM 500 Points Competition** 

By now you have built the base of the application. Its time to add more and complete the existing functionality \


**Important points before you begin:**

**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).

**You are only required to build a subset of the application in this challenge. The rest will be covered in the Hard challenge.**


## What do you need to do in this Medium challenge?

In the easy challenge, you designed the initial screens, we will now move forward and add more interesting screens:

**Homescreen**

This screen will be visible to both authenticated and non-authenticated users.

The Homescreen will look like this:



* The first section will be a centered heading. If the user is not signed, it will say Sign up to start learning If the User is signed it, it will say Join a learning space below
* Below this section will be the following Co-learning Spaces section.
* There will be a grid of ReactJS cards component displaying all co-learning spaces. Each card will display a thumbnail of a co-learning space, the title, number of members and last update in that space. There is no specific design orientation, you are free to design how it looks, but it has to be in a card and with the information mentioned above. More information about card component is mentioned in the reference section.

**Viewing Learning Space**



* When clicked on any co-learning card from the homepage, the app should render that co-learning space screen.
* A learning space shall display an overview of the learning space. 
* A learning space shall display a button to join/leave a learning space near its title. If the user is not authenticated, it should display Sign up to Join otherwise Join
* A learning space shall display a prerequisites list, demonstrating the learning requirements for the learning space. We will cover how this list will appear in the HARD challenge.
* A learning space shall display a list of Posts created inside the learning space in a sorted order by date.
* A learning space shall display a button to create a Post.
* A learning space shall display a list of active users that are currently visiting the learning space. Clicking on one of the users will reroute to their profile page.
* A learning space shall display a list of related learning spaces, which are determined by the keywords used during the creation of learning spaces. We will cover how the keywords are listed in the HARD challenge.


## Recommended learning material

[Cards - React-Bootstrap Documentation](https://react-bootstrap.github.io/components/cards/)

[react-ui-cards - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-ui-cards)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:



   * Create a folder with medium as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to screen record a video of all the functionality you built in this challenge. Upload the video on your google drive and share the link in a text file that you will place in the medium folder.
   * Zip that medium folder and submit to the challenge.

title: Reporting Application - API Development #1
description:
# Challenge Objectives
* The goal of the challenge is to implement REST API with JAVA and MySQL.
* The API will be integrated with React based frontend app in a later challenge.

# Project Background
We are going to build a web application that allows users to download diagnostic reports for a specific field.

The application is supposed to provide several features like:
* Dashboard where users can download diagnostic reports and make inquiries.
* Users can request online consulting services on this application.
* Administrative screens used by support.
* Send email notifications to users.

# Technology Stack
* JAVA（Amazon Corretto 17）
* REST
* Spring Boot 2.7
* MySQL 8
* Auth0

# Challenge Requirements
For this challenge, we are going to build the REST API following the provided API List and database model. Please refresh this page after registering to the challenge to view the API details at the bottom of this page.

# General Requirements
* The provided tech stack must be followed (including versions)
* Must cover all the endpoints in the scope (see the table of endpoints below)
* When implementing each API, follow the Base Code if possible.
* Code needs to be properly designed and structured for easy maintenance (for example: you may want to divide the endpoints into different groups / folders based on the data model)
* Code should be properly documented
* Unit Tests are required.
* The file structure should conform to the standard as much as possible.

# Submission
Submit a single zip file that contains the following:
* The source code. (Any error detected by Lint should be fixed before the submission.)
* A detailed README in markdown format on how to configure, run, and test your submission
* A postman collection that can be used to test the positive and negative cases of the API
* The swagger spec if anything got updated during the challenge
* The sql scripts used to populate the database and/or any master / test data

# API Details
All the following endpoints from swagger are in scope.
## users
* auth0 signup: POST /users/signup
* titan signup: POST /users/me
* get user me: GET /users/me
* put user me: PUT /users/me
* search users: POST /users/search

## admin
* get admin me: GET /admins

## report
* download cra report me: POST /reports/cra/me/latest
* download cra report: POST /reports/cra/me/{id}
* get cra reports: GET /reports/cra/me

## contact
* post contact: POST /contacts

title: Rapid Development Match (RDM) - Practice Round, January 2023
description:
## Overview
This is an old problem, related to TCO20. It is a great example of an RDM style problem. The only difference is that this practice round is *not* rated, and there is only one problem. Normal rated RDM rounds usually have three problems. A practice round is a great way to get started with RDM competitions without risking your rank and without the pressure of the live competition.

You can refer to [this](https://www.topcoder.com/thrive/articles/docker-quickstart-for-rdm-and-automated-testing-challenges) short article for a quick explanation on the submission format.

## Problem description

Bill is an adventurous explorer. One day, he came to a castle named TCO20. It is said that there is a precious treasure in this castle. The treasure is stored in a mysterious box. The only way to get to the treasure is to find the key to unlock the box. Bill looked for clues floor by floor. When he came to the 64th floor underground (Base 64), he found a paper with a bunch of strange symbols:

 `e0MgIjpUMDJleSIia095aXNfX3J1aGl7c3RvZXRfZWsifWVjX3JzeX0=`

Below the symbols there is a note: "13 is your lucky number. You will need to get the JSON value."

![p5r5tBCd3B](https://i.imgur.com/ULzTJfu.png)

**Hint:** Bill is clever, he quickly recognized the following patterns from the note.

- The strange symbols make a base64 encoded string.
- The final deciphered text should be a stringified JSON object.
- The format of the JSON object is `{"key": value}` because we need to decipher the treasure **key**.
- 13 is your lucky number. To decipher the key, maybe we can organize every 13 characters in a row and then rearrange the order of these characters?

## Requirements

**In this challenge, you need to write  web app to help Bill decipher the key from the symbols. We can't tell you the exact decipher steps. You will have to find it by yourself from the background story above and the hints below. **

### Requirements for the app

During the system testing, there will be many other base64 encoded strings that need to be deciphered in the same way, so your code should not just be dedicated to deciphering `e0MgIjpUMDJleSIia095aXNfX3J1aGl7c3RvZXRfZWsifWVjX3JzeX0=`.

# Hints

(1) First, you will need to decode the base64 string.

(2) Then extract the string inside `{}`, and follow the steps below.

(3) Split the message into separate rows. Each row should contain 13 characters. Affirming that when they write the algorithm later to process each row, they can be safely assumed that each row will indeed be of length 13.

(4) Unscramble the first line into human-readable words. That is, rearrange the characters in the first row such that they start making sense. Every character, including space, quotes, braces, etc are important. Do not discard any character. You are expected to only rearrange the characters. You will always rearrange the character within the same row. A character will never be moved to a different row.

(5) Apply for the same rearrangement order from the first line to the remaining lines. 

- Example: If, in the first row, the string is “dPresient”, and you figure out that you need to move the character d (at index 0) to index 5 (so that the “unscrambled” string says President), for ALL the other rows, a character at index 0 will always be moved to index 5.
- Thus, if you correctly figure out the arrangement of characters in the first row, you will automatically figure out the arrangement of the characters in the rest of the rows
Additionally, for EVERY input text, you will follow the same arrangement. That is, if you figure out the arrangement for the provided example, you can use the same rearrangement order for ALL other input and thus translate your understanding into an algorithm that can be easily implemented as code.
- Additionally, for EVERY input text, you will follow the same arrangement. That is, if you figure out the arrangement for the provided example, you can use the same rearrangement order for ALL other input and thus translate your understanding into an algorithm that can be easily implemented as code.

(6). After unscrambling the lines, it will be human-readable text.

(7). Concatenate all the lines into a single string. Enclose this string in `{` and `}` characters. Remember - the output is a JSON object. That will be the output.

### Requirements for the Web App
This page needs to be the index page of your web app and must contains these elements: 

1. Add textarea with id `input`. This textarea is the input where user fill the provided characters. 
2. Add button with text `Decipher` below textarea. Click this button needs to run the deciphering process and return the values to another textarea below
3. Add another textarea with id `output` below Decipher button. This textarea is where user see the deciphering result. 


It will help the automated-tester to validate your submission.

# Constraints & Guidelines

- Please use the forums to ask any questions.
- Minimal code quality is expected for the reviewer to review the submission and go through your code
- Obvious and deliberate code obfuscation will be rejected.
- Collaborating/Cheating in any way with anyone else (member or not) during a rated event is considered cheating.
- An excessive amount of unused content should be avoided.
- Code Readability is expected with necessary comments, indentation, and somewhat refactored code (as much as possible)

## Technology Stack

- Programming language: No limitation
- Docker

## Submission Deliverables

Submit a zip file. Inside the zip file, there needs to be a folder name code. Inside the code folder, you need to have a Dockerfile which we will use to build the image and start your application by starting a container using the image. The code folder can contain other folders and files but it needs to contain the Dockerfile. The zip file too can contain other folders and files, but it needs to have the code folder at the ROOT.

## Sample submissions

* Python/Flash [code_python.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1U-CSRr008s9FmK4L6WOT3O5RMNQ_m12B/view?usp=share_link)
* ExpressJS [code_node.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ru6KCvUwX_VMQRNLpVzXHz7SUvCrKLxU/view?usp=share_link)
* Tester for testing your solution locally [sample-and-tester.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WoMDBOdIczHobG-uDsOWBAA4kJCzMCCY/view?usp=share_link)

title: Reporting Application - Thymeleaf Based HTML Email Templates
description:
# Challenge/Task Objectives
Build Thymeleaf-based HTML templates to be used in the emails the application sends.

# Project Background
We are going to build a web application that allows users to download diagnostic reports for a specific field.
The application is supposed to provide several features like;
* Dashboard where users can download diagnostic reports and make inquiries.
* Users can request online consulting services on this application.
* Administrative screens used by support.
* Send email notifications to users.

# Technology Stack
* HTML
* CSS
* Thymeleaf

# Challenge Requirements
For this challenge, we are going to build the HTML for the email that covers the following emails:
* R002: Account Create Completion
* R006: Contact Email (Support)
* R007: Contact Email (User)
* R010: Assessment Update Notification
* R022: Assessment Complete Notification
* R026: Assessment Complete Notification (Agency)

You can refer to the XD file of each email screen after registration.

# General Requirements
* Must develop the HTML email templates based on Thymeleaf
* HTML with responsive design for mobile mail app, client mail software and web client
* Follow HTML email development best practices as recommended in https://www.emailonacid.com/blog/article/email-development/email-development-best-practices-2/ and https://templates.mailchimp.com/getting-started/html-email-basics/
* The main content should have fluid width
* No errors with production build
* You are expected to create a detailed README file including information on how to setup, run and verify your application

# HTML Requirements
* HTML should be valid HTML5 compliant.
* Provide comments on the page elements to give a clear explanation of the code usage. The goal is to help future developers understand the code.
* Please use clean INDENTATION for all HTML code so future developers can follow the code.
* All HTML code naming should not have any conflicts or errors.
* Element and Attribute names should be in lowercase and use a "-" or camel naming to separate multiple-word classes (i.e.. "main-content", or "mainContent)
* Use semantically correct tags- use H tags for headers, etc. Use strong and em tags instead of bold and italic tags.
* Validate your code- reviewers may accept minor validation errors, but please comment your reason for any validation errors. Use the validators listed in the scorecard.

# Licenses & Attribution
Third-party assets used to build your item (likely not needed at all for this F2F) must be properly licensed and free for commercial use. MIT and Apache v2 licenses are ok, for any others please check with us to get approval first Sufficient information regarding third-party assets must be present in your documentation. This includes the author, license info and a direct link to the asset online.

# Submission
Submit a single zip file that contains all the Thymeleaf-based HTML email templates and a detailed README in markdown format.

title: Phase 1: AI Agent for Innovation: From Idea to Live Challenge
description:
This is Phase 1 of a 3-part program to build an **AI Copilot Agent** that helps users define and launch structured innovation or development challenges on platforms like **Topcoder, HeroX, Kaggle, Zindi**, or internal company systems.

Your mission is to implement the **reasoning and guidance engine** of the Copilot — the backend AI logic that interacts with a user to understand their goal, assess its feasibility for a challenge, clarify details, and ultimately construct a well-scoped, structured spec ready for submission.

The AI agent must **ask dynamic, adaptive questions**, guide scoping with reasoning, and refine its behavior based on user answers and platform requirements. No hardcoded scripts — the system should **reason, recall, and respond contextually**.

---

## Objectives

Your Copilot must be able to:

1. Accept a high-level user input (e.g. “I want to build an app for cleaning services”)
2. Initiate a **scoping dialogue**:
   * Ask contextual questions about project status
   * Clarify whether this is design, development, testing, or PoC
   * Suggest possible scoping directions
   * Let the user confirm or reject them
   * Reassess scope feasibility iteratively
3. Once scope is confirmed, **load a dynamic schema** (platform-specific challenge fields)
4. Drive structured Q\&A to complete the spec fields
5. Retrieve similar challenges via RAG to inform questions and structure
6. Output a **structured, reasoned spec** (JSON or similar)
7. Store and adapt to user feedback per section or suggestion

---

## How Scoping Should Work

The Copilot is not allowed to impose a scope.

Instead, it should:

1. **Evaluate scope feasibility**
   Example prompt:
   > "This seems like a broad goal. Can you tell me what part is currently your priority, or what’s already done?"
2. **Ask project clarifying questions**
   * “What stage is the project in?”
   * “Do you have mockups or designs?”
   * “Is this the first task, or part of a series of challenges?”
3. **Propose possible scoped ideas** based on the user's reply, e.g.:
   > “Based on what you shared, we might start with designing the booking screen. Does that work for you?”
   * If user says yes → continue
   * If user says no → ask what they prefer, then re-evaluate if that fits challenge boundaries (timeline, cost, deliverable size)
4. **Repeat the cycle** until both agent and user agree on a scoped unit of work
5. **Then start compiling the spec** with dynamic field-driven questions

---

## Use of Agent Frameworks

You must use an existing AI agent orchestration framework:

Recommended:
* **LangGraph**
* **CrewAI**
* **AutoGen**
* **SuperAgent**
* **Flowise**

You may also use **OpenAI Assistants API** if it includes:
* Memory across turns
* Function calling
* Tool-based interaction

---

## Dynamic Field Schema (Configurable)

Challenge requirements vary per platform. Your Copilot must:

* Load challenge field definitions dynamically (JSON, YAML, or API)
* Use them to:
  * Ask relevant questions
  * Track checklist progress
  * Format the final output

Example (suggested, not required):

```json
{
  "platform": "Topcoder",
  "challenge_type": "Design",
  "fields": {
    "title": { "required": true },
    "overview": { "required": true },
    "objectives": { "required": true },
    "tech_stack": { "required": false },
    "timeline": { "required": true },
    "prize_structure": { "required": true },
    "judging_criteria": { "required": true }
  }
}
```

Fields must not be hardcoded. Make the schema **configurable per platform and challenge type**.

---

## RAG Integration

Integrate a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline:
* Use a vector database (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Qdrant)
* Store past challenge specs, templates, and suggestions
* Retrieve relevant examples based on user topic, category, or phase
* Use these as grounding for:
  * Question phrasing
  * Estimating timeline
  * Suggesting judging criteria
  * Platform-specific formatting

---

## Feedback Loop Handling

As users answer questions or reject suggestions:
* Capture their feedback (e.g., “React won’t work for us”)
* Adapt future prompts during the session to avoid repetition
* Log reasoning traces for all suggestions
* If rejected → ask why → re-suggest

Example:

```json
{
  "field": "tech_stack",
  "ai_suggestion": "React",
  "accepted": false,
  "reason": "We prefer Flutter for cross-platform",
  "prompt_update": true
}
```

---

## Output Format (Structured Spec + Reasoning)

Final spec output should include:
* Structured challenge fields (e.g., JSON or object)
* Reasoning per field (why it was filled this way)
* Confidence level (optional)

Example:

```json
{
  "title": "Cleaner Booking Flow - UI Prototype",
  "overview": "Design 3 screens for customer booking in a cleaning app",
  "tech_stack": ["Figma"],
  "timeline": { "submission_days": 6 },
  "reasoning_trace": [
    {
      "field": "tech_stack",
      "source": "User confirmed mockup design only; no code needed",
      "confidence": 0.92
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Tech Stack
* **LLM**: OpenAI GPT-4 (with Functions or Assistants API)
* **Agent Framework**: LangGraph (preferred) or CrewAI
* **Prompt Routing & Memory**: LangChain (optional if using LangGraph)
* **RAG Framework**: LangChain or LlamaIndex
* **Vector Store**: Qdrant (preferred), Pinecone, or FAISS (local dev)
* **Embeddings**: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or Instructor-XL

2. 3 end-to-end session logs (user → Copilot → spec)

   * Agent orchestration setup
   * Memory handling
   * Prompt strategies
   * Schema configurability

---

## Bonus Points
You’ll earn bonus points in functionality and UI evaluation criterias for:

* Vision model support (upload mockups for context)
* ⚛**React frontend integration** (we provide the UI shell):
  * Chat interface
  * Checklist view
  * Challenge spec viewer

title: Topcoder Review API - Design Prisma Schema & OpenAPI Spec for Submissions API Migration
description:
This series of challenges focus on migrating the existing Submissions API functionality into the Topcoder Review API. The goal is to ensure all Submissions API endpoints are available in the new Topcoder Review API with identical request/response structures to maintain backward compatibility with existing services that depend on the Submissions API.

This challenge focuses on designing the database schema (using Prisma ORM) and OpenAPI documentation (via NestJS) for the migrated Submission API.

### Background

The current Submissions API handles several key functions including:

- Submission management (create, read, update, delete)
- Review management
- Review types management
- Review summations

As per the requirements, we need to integrate these functions into the new Review API while maintaining the same endpoint structure and behavior to avoid updating dependent services.

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **ID Format**: nanoID (to maintain compatibility with the new Review API)
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api)
- Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

### Detailed Requirements

#### 1. Prisma Schema Extensions

Extend the existing Prisma schema to include models for the Submissions API:

**Important: Avoid duplication with existing models in the Review API.** The Review API already has some overlapping models and functionality, particularly with reviews. You should:

1. **Identify overlapping models** - The Review API already contains a **review** model with similar functionality to the Submissions API's Review model.
2. **Extend existing models** - Where models overlap, extend the existing Review API models rather than creating new ones.
3. **Create new models** - Only create new models for functionality that doesn't exist in the Review API.
4. **Maintain compatibility** - Ensure that the existing Review API functionality continues to work while adding Submissions API compatibility.

Models to evaluate for integration:

- **Review** - The Review API already has a **review** model. Extend this model to support the Submissions API review functionality rather than creating a new model.
- **Submission** - Create a new model if not already present in the Review API.
- **ReviewType** - Evaluate if this can be integrated with the existing ScorecardType enum or if a new model is needed.
- **ReviewSummation** - Create a new model if not already present in the Review API.

### 2. API Endpoints

Implement the following endpoints with identical request/response structures as the existing Submissions API. 

**For endpoints that overlap with existing Review API functionality, create adapter layers that maintain the original Submissions API interface while using the Review API's underlying functionality.**

#### Submission Endpoints

- **POST /submissions** - Create a new submission
- **GET /submissions** - List submissions with filtering
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId** - Get a specific submission
- **PUT /submissions/:submissionId** - Update a submission
- **PATCH /submissions/:submissionId** - Partially update a submission
- **DELETE /submissions/:submissionId** - Delete a submission
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/download** - Download submission file
- **POST /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts** - Add artifact to submission
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts** - List artifacts for a submission
- **DELETE /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts/:file** - Delete an artifact
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts/:file/download** - Download an artifact
- **GET /submissions/:challengeId/count** - Count submissions for a challenge

#### Review Endpoints

- **POST /reviews** - Create a new review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviews** - List reviews with filtering (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviews/:reviewId** - Get a specific review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **PUT /reviews/:reviewId** - Update a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **PATCH /reviews/:reviewId** - Partially update a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **DELETE /reviews/:reviewId** - Delete a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)

#### ReviewType Endpoints

- **POST /reviewTypes** - Create a new review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviewTypes** - List review types (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Get a specific review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **PUT /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Update a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **PATCH /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Partially update a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **DELETE /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Delete a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)

#### ReviewSummation Endpoints

-  **POST /reviewSummations** - Create a new review summation
-  **GET /reviewSummations** - List review summations
- **GET /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Get a specific review summation
- **PUT /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Update a review summation
- **PATCH /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Partially update a review summation
- **DELETE /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Delete a review summation

### 3. New Functionality

Implement the new functionality as specified in the requirements:

- **GET /submissions/download/:challengeId** - Download all submissions for a challenge as a ZIP file

## Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema with new models
   - NestJS controllers, services, and DTOs for all endpoints
   - OpenAPI documentation for all endpoints

2. A design document (in markdown format) explaining:
   - The integration approach
   - Mapping between old and new data models
   - Authentication and authorization strategy
   - Any deviations from the original API (if necessary)

3. A deployment guide (in markdown format).

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api) as the code base.
- Ensure Swagger docs are accessible at http://localhost:3000/api-docs.
- The goal is to have a seamless transition from the old Submissions API to the new Review API without requiring changes to dependent services.

title: [72hr] TopCoder Member API: Prisma Schema Update
description:
## Project Overview

The current Member API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the Member API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

This challenge focuses on updating the database schema (using Prisma ORM) and focus on the JSON field re-design.

## Requirements
### Prisma Schema Update
All the JSON fields are in scope of this challenge:
- model member: maxRating, addresses, aggregatedSkills and enteredSkills
- model distributionStats: distribution
- model memberHistoryStats: DEVELOP and DATA_SCIENCE
- model memberStats: maxRating, DEVELOP, DESIGN, DATA_SCIENCE and COPILOT
- model memberTrait: traits

#### traits
We have a formal set of traits we support:
- Devices (see https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Software (see https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Work (see Experience on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Education (see Education and Certifications on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Onboarding Checklist (see response from `v5/members/phead/traits?` on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Basic info (see response from `v5/members/phead/traits?` on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Languages (Set on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Communities (Set on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tcandyou)
- Personalization (currently just the user consent flag on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#account)

Let's define sub-objects / tables for each trait, instead of just using JSON.  If we ever want to add new traits, we'll update the schema with a new trait table, instead of storing JSON.

These categories are deprecated:
- Connect User Information

#### maxRating, DEVELOP, DESIGN, DATA_SCIENCE and COPILOT
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/phead/stats/Development
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/lX3MwSHT16t3oOsXSuDR)

#### distribution
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/phead/stats/Development/CODE
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/D5rcaxSRTRuhGKcyLz4j)

#### addresses
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/{your_id}

#### aggregatedSkills and enteredSkills
- Let's keep these two as JSON type for now.

## Technology Stack

- Backend: NodeJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger

## Resources
- Member API code: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop
- Prisma: [https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma)
- Sample Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

## Submission Guidelines

- updated Prisma schema file.
- updated Swagger file.
- A readme file explaining the transformation from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema and verification.

## Judging Criteria:

- Completeness of all transformations from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema
- Clarity and accuracy of OpenAPI documentation.
- Adherence to Prisma best practices (e.g., indexes, naming).

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

title: Topcoder Review App - API Integration Part 1
description:
The Topcoder Review App is currently a UI prototype using mock data in the platform-ui repository. This challenge focuses on integrating real API functionality into the Review App, specifically for the ActiveReviewsPage and ChallengeDetailsPage components. 

The existing Review API (tc-review-api) will need to be enhanced to properly integrate with Challenge API and Resources API for the Active Reviews Page. For the Challenge Details Page, the Review App frontend (platform-ui) will directly integrate with multiple API services to fetch and aggregate data.

### Technology Stack

- Frontend:
  - React
  - TypeScript
  - SCSS + CSS Modules

- Backend:
  - Node.js
  - NestJS
  - Prisma ORM
  - PostgreSQL

### Assets

- Review App (platform-ui): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui
- Review API (tc-review-api): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git
- Challenge API (challenge-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git
- Resources API (resource-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6.git

### Individual Requirements

#### 1. API Integration for ActiveReviewsPage (Active Reviews Page)

##### 1.1 Backend Enhancement (tc-review-api)

Update the tc-review-api's **GET /api/reviews** endpoint to integrate with Challenge API and Resources API. The current endpoint doesn't fetch the correct pending reviews for users with different roles.

Implementation Requirements:
- Enhance the **ReviewController.getPendingReviews()** method to:
  - Utilize the existing **ChallengeApiService** in tc-review-api to call Challenge API's **GET /v5/challenges/:challengeId** to check if challenges are still active
  - Create a new **ResourceApiService** to call Resources API's **GET /resources?challengeId=:challengeId** to get the user's roles for challenges
  - Filter reviews based on the user's role and challenge status
  - Return properly formatted response including user role information


##### 1.2 Frontend Update (platform-ui)

Update the ActiveReviewsPage component and related services to fetch data from the real API:

Implementation Requirements:
- Replace mock data in **fetchActiveReviews()** function with real API calls to **tc-review-api**'s enhanced endpoint
- Update the **useFetchActiveReviews()** hook to handle API response format
- Add appropriate error handling and loading states
- Preserve the existing filter functionality for challenge types
- Add support for displaying the user's role for each challenge in the ActiveReviews table

Code Updates:
- Update **reviews.service.ts** to make real API calls instead of using mock data
- Remove references to mock data from **useFetchActiveReviews.ts**
- Update **TableActiveReviews** component to display user role information
- Add proper loading state indicators and error handling

#### 2. API Integration for ChallengeDetailsPage (Challenge Review Details Page)

##### 2.1 Frontend Direct API Integration (platform-ui)

Instead of creating a new backend endpoint, implement direct API integration with multiple services in the platform-ui:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create new service modules in platform-ui to communicate with each API:
  - **challenge-api.service.ts**: To call Challenge API endpoints:
    - **GET /v5/challenges/:challengeId** - Get challenge details
    - **GET /v5/challenge-phases** - Get phase information
    - **GET /v5/challenge-timelines/:challengeTimelineTemplateId** - Get timeline templates
  
  - **resources-api.service.ts**: To call Resources API endpoints:
    - **GET /resources?challengeId=:challengeId** - Get resources assigned to the challenge
    - **GET /resource-roles** - Get resource role information
  
  - Update **reviews.service.ts** to call tc-review-api endpoints:
    - **GET /api/reviews?challengeId=:challengeId** - Get reviews for the challenge
    - **GET /api/project-result/:challengeId** - Get project results

- Create a data orchestration layer that:
  - Executes parallel API calls to all required endpoints
  - Aggregates data from multiple sources
  - Filters and transforms data based on user role
  - Provides a coherent data model for the UI components

Code Updates:
- Create new API service modules in **src/apps/review/src/lib/services/**
- Update **useFetchChallengeInfo()** hook to orchestrate multiple API calls
- Implement data aggregation logic in a utility function or service
- Update **ChallengeDetailsPage.tsx** to handle real API data
- Remove all related mock data and references

##### 2.2 Role-Based Display Logic

Implement proper conditional rendering based on user role:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create role-based filters to display appropriate data:
  - For members / submitters: Display only their own submission with all reviews
  - For reviewers: Display all submissions with only their own reviews
  - For copilots / admins: Display all submissions with all reviews that they managed.
- Update the tabs display logic to show appropriate tabs based on user role and challenge phase
- Implement proper permission checks for displaying/editing data

#### 3. Shared API Integration Tasks

##### 3.1 Authentication and Error Handling

Implement proper authentication and error handling:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create an auth service that:
  - Retrieves and stores authentication tokens
  - Adds authorization headers to API requests
  - Handles token refresh when needed
- Implement comprehensive error handling:
  - Add proper loading indicators for API calls in progress
  - Display user-friendly error messages
  - Implement retry mechanisms where appropriate
  - Handle partial data loading scenarios

##### 3.2 API Service Architecture

Create a clean API service architecture in platform-ui:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create a base API service class with common functionality:
  - Token management
  - Error handling
  - Request/response logging
- Extend this base class for each specific API integration
- Implement proper TypeScript interfaces for all API responses
- Add caching for API responses to improve performance

### Important Notes

1. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

2. Remove related mock data and references after implementing real API integration. Update imports and dependencies accordingly.

3. The tc-review-api code should be updated ONLY for the ActiveReviewsPage integration with the enhanced /api/reviews endpoint. No new endpoints need to be created in tc-review-api.

4. For the ChallengeDetailsPage, all API integration should be done directly in platform-ui.

5. Leverage the existing **ChallengeApiService** in tc-review-api as a reference for implementing API clients. Create a similar **ResourceApiService** for calling the Resources API.

6. Make sure to implement proper loading states and error handling for all API integrations.

7. All API calls should be properly typed using TypeScript interfaces.

8. Implement appropriate caching strategies for API responses to improve performance and reduce API calls.

9. Challenge API (challenge-api-v6) and Resources API (resource-api-v6) contain all the endpoints needed for this integration. No modifications to these services are required.

### Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:

1. Git patches against the specified branch in both repositories:
   - tc-review-api: the feat/review-opportunities of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git
   - platform-ui: the feat/review branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui 

2. Documentation (in markdown) explaining:
   - New service modules created in platform-ui for direct API integration
   - Data aggregation strategy implemented on the frontend
   - How to test the integration

title: Topcoder Admin App - Misc Update 0610

title: Test123
Fix Signature Handling in Outlook Add-in
description:
This F2F challenge aims to resolve two issues in the `office-js` GitHub repo related to the behavior of **custom signatures** in Outlook add-ins during reply/forward and signature modification workflows.

* GitHub Issue 1: [#5803](https://github.com/OfficeDev/office-js/issues/5803) – Signature not loading correctly in inline replies/forwards.
* GitHub Issue 2: [#5804](https://github.com/OfficeDev/office-js/issues/5804) – Signature modification does not trigger smart alert.

---

### Goals

1. **Issue #5803**: Ensure that custom signatures load automatically when replying or forwarding **inline** (not just in pop-out).
2. **Issue #5804**: Ensure that modifying the signature (e.g., adding text) **triggers the Smart Alerts** dialog to reflect content changes.

---

### Reproduction Steps (for Verification)

#### Issue #5803 Repro

1. Install a custom Outlook add-in that injects a signature via `setSignatureAsync`.
2. Reply or forward an email **inline**.
3. Observe that the signature is **not inserted** unless the compose window is popped out.

#### Issue #5804 Repro

1. Trigger custom signature insertion via the add-in.
2. Edit the signature area (e.g., add some text).
3. Observe that **no Smart Alert** appears to indicate the signature has been modified.


---

### Deliverables

* Modified sample add-in (manifest + code) demonstrating fixed behavior.
* Any code patches (or recommended API changes if outside of add-in scope).
* Demo videos
* Short documentation (README or comments) describing root cause and fix strategy.

You can find the existing code at: https://github.com/mirzailhami/outlook-signature-add-ins/tree/dev-mobile-graph-classic

title: Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource** and **GroupResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to migrate  resource  **AuthorizationResource** and fix several issues in previous challenge.

## Detail Requirements
- The endpoints need to be considered to migrate is **AuthorizationResource**;
-  Keep features in the Identity Service, such as **redis** as cache, **Kafka** message sending through the event bus, **Shiro-like** permission check ;
- Make sure to use two data sources: one for tables currently exist in **common_oltp** database  from **Postgres**, another for the rest of the data in **MySQL**.
- Make sure that the migration can happen and people can still log in. We should migrate the passwords without having to do anything - they are encrypted in the DB and currently what are used for authentication;
- Write unit tests for these endpoints in AuthorizationResource and make sure **test coverage >= 90%**%;
- Write or update postman test collection for these endpoints in AuthorizationResource;
- Update Swagger file properly for each apis in AuthorizationResource;
- Fix the following issues from previous code:
	- **Remove all Dice related code and text since we are deprecating Dice ID as the 2FA mechanism for Topcoder login, we are instead going to replace it with standard Auth0 2FA, like a normal code generator.**
	- define a constant like following to replace all the specified constants in the code(group.controller.ts, group.service.ts):				
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/i8bgSVlfRGyn6BRiXV2f)
	- validateMembership should be handled properly. In original code, validateMembership validates groupMembership.getMembershipTypeId is null or not, but in the revised code, it’s checking groupMembership.getMembershipType which is different.
- Update **README** file on how to set up instruction, how to test, how to verify;

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

title: Topcoder Challenge API - Issues Fix 0613
description:
### Project overview

The current challenge  API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves

So, we are aiming to revise the challenge API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Challenge  API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/7dc44b10-2e27-4e57-bec1-4891c34aec4a) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Challenge API: Data Migration Tool to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/22477223-2354-449d-a3b5-2d4d63c4cb25)

title: Topcoder Marathon Match Scorer Rebuild POC Part 2 Relaunched
description:
### Project overview
Currently, We have a single large EC2 instance that runs all the time. The EC2 instance runs a simple processor that handles Kafka messages to a few queues,
When a submission comes in, the Kafka message is sent by the submission API and the processor picks up docker process to score the submission back to submission API. 

Docker hub isn't ideal for our platform purposes.  It would be much better if the compiled scorer lived in ECR, which is where all our production code is sent and then deployed via Fargate / ECS.

We'd like to get away from an always-running EC2 instance for the scorer since that's expensive and unnecessary when it's just not scoring or doing anything (like right now between MMs).  Instead, scoring will be on-demand for each submission coming in.

We have done the first challenge of this series which identified the new workflow with fake scorer. We'd like to explore the implementation of scorer in this challenge.

### Individual Requirements
In this challenge, we need to replace the docker scorer to Fargate scorer. We may need move the common code into the Fargate scorer implementation so that we can change the configuration and actual tester class/code when do the testing for each challenge.

**Notes: LET"S ONLY FOCUS ON THE JAVA SCORER.**

#### Scorer Configuration
1. Configuration or environment variables could be moved to Parameter Store. Check com.topcoder.marathon.Parameters for reference. We can change the configuration between the challenges easily without reach out the code.
1. We'll need a way to configure the scorer to use for each challenge ID.  Note that the current Docker scorer (tc-mm-processor) has a configuration value for the challenge ID to "watch" and process submissions for.
```
 // Check for expected challengeId
    if (!challengeId || challengeId !== targetedChallenge) {
      logger.info(
        `Ignoring message as challengeId - ${challengeId} is not of interest`
      )58=55202
      return
    }
```
We'll want a way to do multiple challenges and multiple scorers, with the potential for multiple scorers to run for a single challenge ID as well.  For instance, on MM 161, we have example scoring and provisional scoring for each submission, which is two separate EC2 instances (one running execute.sh on each submission and one running executeExample.sh on each submission).  Currently, in addition to the different scorer / review types, if we have concurrent MMs running, we just run a whole separate EC2 instance for each challenge, where each instance is dedicated to a single scorer, which isn't efficient.

3. Refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-parameter-store.html about how to read values from Parameter Store.

#### The structure of the scorer
I have attached a completed scorer tc-mm-160. Basically, there are two part of the scorer:
1. marathon, ReviewHelper and ReviewTest are almost same between different challenges.
2. BioSlimeTester.java: this is the test code for the specific challenge. 

#### The output of the scorer
* In current implementation of scorer, the score of the submission reads from file (L157 - L162 in KafkaHandlerService.js), we should design a way to send back to main lambda function.
* After receive the score, the main lambda function should create a review through review api.

#### The Submission Lifecycle
We'd like to introduce following status to the submissions:
1. submitted
2. running examples
3. running provisional
4. completed

So when the submissions coming, we need to mark the status to submitted, and then run the examples and provisional scorers. When the process finished, it need to mark the status to completed.

### Submission
Your submission should include:

* A zip file contains all updated codes.
* A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
* A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new scorer functionality

title: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot: Event-Based Phase Transition Implementation
description:
The Topcoder Autopilot service is responsible for automatically transitioning challenges through their phases at the appropriate times. In [the first challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30376827/?type=develop), we established the core project infrastructure and integrated with Kafka for event messaging. This second challenge focuses on implementing an event-based mechanism to replace the current polling approach, making the system more efficient and responsive.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Challenge Background

The current Autopilot-v6 codebase has established the Kafka infrastructure for sending and receiving events but lacks the core scheduling functionality. While the system can manually trigger events, there's no automated scheduling mechanism to trigger them at specific phase end times. This challenge aims to implement this functionality using NestJS's scheduling capabilities.

### Codebase

- [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git)

### Individual Requirements

#### 1. Event-Based Scheduling Mechanism

Implement a dynamic scheduling system using NestJS's @nestjs/schedule module:

- Add the @nestjs/schedule module to the project dependencies
- Create a scheduler service that leverages SchedulerRegistry to manage dynamic job registration and cancellation
- Implement methods to schedule one-time tasks based on phase end times
- Each scheduled job should correspond to a specific phase end event

#### 2. Event Generation

Enhance the existing Kafka infrastructure to automatically generate events:

- At the scheduled time, trigger phase transition events
- Publish these events to the appropriate Kafka topic (autopilot.phase.transition)
- Ensure the event payload includes challenge ID, phase ID, and other relevant metadata as per the existing message format

#### 3. Schedule Adjustment Handling

Implement logic to handle changes to phase schedules:

- Create handlers for challenge/phase updates that might affect scheduled end times
- Implement logic to cancel existing scheduled jobs when phase end times are changed
- Re-schedule events with updated timing information
- Ensure all schedule changes are properly logged for audit purposes

#### 4. Resilience and Recovery

Implement mechanisms to ensure the system can recover from failures:

- Create a recovery mechanism that executes when the service restarts
- On startup, scan all active phases and schedule events for upcoming transitions
- Handle edge cases like immediate phase transitions (end times in the past)
- Implement proper error handling and logging throughout the scheduling process

#### 5. Documentation

- Document the scheduling approach and recovery mechanisms
- Include examples of how the system handles different scenarios (normal scheduling, rescheduling, recovery)

### Implementation Details

#### Scheduler Service

Create a new service that will:

1. Use the SchedulerRegistry from @nestjs/schedule to manage dynamic jobs
2. Provide methods to:
   - Schedule a phase transition event
   - Cancel a scheduled phase transition
   - Update a scheduled phase transition
   - Get all scheduled transitions

Example interface (Just for your reference):

```typescript
interface SchedulerService {
  schedulePhaseTransition(phaseData: PhaseTransitionScheduleDto): Promise<string>; // returns job id
  cancelScheduledTransition(jobId: string): Promise<boolean>;
  updateScheduledTransition(jobId: string, phaseData: PhaseTransitionScheduleDto): Promise<string>; // returns new job id
  getAllScheduledTransitions(): Promise<ScheduledTransitionInfo[]>;
}
```

#### Phase Transition Handler

Enhance the existing AutopilotService to:

1. Process phase transition requests
2. Schedule phase transitions based on end times
3. Handle updates to phase schedules
4. Trigger the appropriate Kafka events when transitions occur

#### Recovery Mechanism

Implement a recovery service that:

1. Runs on application startup
2. Fetches all active phases from the challenge service
3. Schedules transitions for phases that haven't ended yet
4. Immediately processes phases that should have already ended

### Important Notes

1. The existing Kafka infrastructure should be leveraged for event publishing
2. All scheduled jobs should be properly managed to prevent memory leaks
3. The solution should be designed with scalability in mind, potentially allowing for distributed scheduling in the future
4. Proper error handling and logging are essential for debugging and monitoring 
5. All required features are properly implemented
6. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

### Submission Guideline

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git), which should include

title: Topcoder Ratings Processor - Rebuild with Postgres Part 1
description:
## Project Overview
Currently the Ratings Processor updates the long_comp_result and algo_rating tables in Informix. long_comp_result is fine, but the algo_rating table doesn’t properly store the history for the ratings updates, making backing out any ratings updates that were done incorrectly impossible. The new ratings processor needs to be designed to be more robust, allowing us to run ratings directly for specific challenges, potentially out of sequence.

So, we are aiming to revise the Ratings Processor to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.

This challenge focuses on **re-implements ratings processor using Postgres with Prisma**. 

## Requirements
1. All event handlers are in scope:
- processRegistration
- processReview
- processReviewSummation
- processReviewEnd

2. Some updates on current implementation:
- round id is out of date, prefer to use challenge id and legacy challenge id as the track key.
- mock a service to get ratings and vol instead of fetching it from algo_rating table.

3. General Notes:
- Update the code to remove logic with Informix and adhere to the Prisma schema; the **business logic should not change**;
- After updating the code, we should only have dependency on PostgreSQL as database;
-  Update the Readme on how to configure, run and verify the code;
-  No code lint errors;

NOTE: Ask questions in the forum if anything is unclear —do not assume functionality!
## Technology Stack

- Backend: NodeJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Postgres version **16.3**

## Resources
- tc-ratings-processor code: (https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-ratings-processor/tree/develop)

## Unit tests
Any existing tests are expected to pass as they do now - we can't break any tests that are currently passing.

## Submission Guidelines

✅ All your code.
✅ Seed data for testing: Any required data for testing and validating your submission should be included.
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and test details.

## Judging Criteria:

- Completeness of all transformations from Informix to Prisma schema
- Adherence to NodeJS best practices

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .
- Winner will need to **do final fix** and push his submission to new repository.

title: Topcoder Resource API - add support for memberId for resource API
description:
## Project Overview

The current challenge API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the **resources API** to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/77910b35-7ad7-4e67-a953-85d88b7d6cf6) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Data Migration Tool From Dynamo to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/f0cc9d2f-e2c4-48f6-9381-ccfb92330773)

The goal for this F2F challenge is to add support for memberId in resource API.

# Individual Requirements
- Currently the resource API uses `memberHandle`, which can be changed,  to identify a user. Thus, we need to add `memberId` as a new approach to identify a user. In the meantime, we still need to support `memberHandle` as existing code still uses it.
There are two specified APIs involved shown as below (If new API is found, it's also in scope.):
https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/blob/6ed0e24f07099f1e69ae9cc2baadf272edeedddd/src/services/ResourceService.js#L369
https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/blob/6ed0e24f07099f1e69ae9cc2baadf272edeedddd/src/services/ResourceService.js#L452
- make sure `memberHandle` or `memberId` is passed in the request body. Make sure to use `memberid` firstly, then  `memberHandle`. 
- Update all APIs to set `createdBy` and `updatedBy` as `memberId`, not `memberHandle`.
- Fix the tests if they're failed due to changes in code;
# Resources
- Resource API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/tree/develop
- Migration Data: Will provide in forum

# Submission
Your submission should include:

A patch file against the develop branch of resource API
A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the functionality
