title: Topcoder Identity Service: fix authentication issue
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookuap roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're going to fix authentication issue presented in the new code.

## Detail Requirements

1. Fix the authentication issue:
 We find a request to authenticate a known user in dev (with the v6 service configured with the same blowfish key as the legacy service) doesn't work.
 If I encrypt a plain text password using the configuration in the Identity service (UserService.encodePasswordLegacy), using the same key as the legacy service, I get a different result than what's stored in the database for the users.
When do authentication, the new code tries to decrypt the password in the DB to compare against the plain text password coming in. This logic is inconsistent with original logic.
See the logic in the new code https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/blob/0c41db577fb184589bd48022be6877e0e42e25e5/src/api/user/auth-flow.service.ts#L1009-L1010
and compare that with logic in the old code https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/dao/UserDAO.java#L817-L818

 Basicially, we should be consistent with the old code, which is shown as follows:
 1. Get the current, encrypted, password from the DB
 2. Encrypt the provided password using Blowfish
 3. Compare the encrypted provided password with the encrypted password from the DB
 4. If they match, allow login, otherwise reject

The above issue exists in the following methods which need to be fixed and test through postman tests:
 - user.controller.ts#getOneTimeToken: the logic should be matching https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/UserResource.java#L2036
 - user.controller.ts#auth0Login : the logic should be matching https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/c5d95c78ef6e50eca64e2f808ac10fc075fa0b4f/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/UserResource.java#L788

2. Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to verify through postman collections;
3. Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

title: Topcoder Identity Service: feature improvements
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to use single Postgres schema and remove group related tables.

## Detail Requirements

- Remove the dependency of `Crypto.JS` as it's depcrecated, and pease use `crypto`. There are mainly two services involved: `UserService`, and `AuthFlowService`. see details here https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/4
- Remove `Group` and `GroupMembership` tables, instead using Group Service v6. Details can be seen here https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/3
- Remove `common_oltp` and `authorization` schemas and use a single `identity` schema. See https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/issues/1
- Currently, there is only one postman test for each api which is not enough, thus, rewrite postman collections for each api to cover every cases. This will be considered as a **MAJOR REQUIREMENT**.
- Changes made should be tested thouroughly, so that the functionalities are consistent with the old java code.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.
- Tests are out of scope.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

Topcoder Review API - Legacy Submissions Data Import for Submissions API Migration - REPOST
description:
This challenge focuses on migrating historical submissions data from Topcoder's legacy system to the new Prisma-based schema in the Topcoder Review API. In [the previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377016/?type=develop), we successfully migrated the Submissions API endpoints into the Review API while maintaining backward compatibility. Now, we need to import the legacy submissions data to ensure these endpoints return real data instead of mock responses.

The data to be imported comes from two sources:
1. Legacy Informix database exports (JSON format)
2. ElasticSearch exports from the current Submissions API

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **ID Format**: nanoID (to maintain compatibility with the Review API)
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api)
- Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

### Core Requirements:

#### 1. Data Transformation Rules

**1.1 Submissions (Informix):**

- Convert legacy submission records to the new Prisma schema format
- Map submission status IDs to the appropriate enum values
- Map submission type IDs to the appropriate enum values
- Preserve all metadata including scores, placements, and timestamps
- Ensure proper relationships between submissions and related entities (uploads, users, challenges)

**1.2 Uploads (Informix):**

- Transform upload records to the new schema format
- Map upload status IDs to appropriate enum values
- Map upload type IDs to appropriate enum values
- Maintain relationships between uploads and submissions

**1.3 Submission Artifacts (Informix):**

- Import submission artifacts data if available
- Ensure proper relationships between submissions and their artifacts

**1.4 ElasticSearch Data:**

- Import data from ElasticSearch exports (dev-submissions-api.data.json)
- Map ElasticSearch document fields to the appropriate Prisma schema fields
- Handle different document types (reviews, submissions, etc.)
- Preserve all metadata and relationships

**1.5 Review Migration Error Fix**

- Fix the review migration errors, particularly for review records with undefined error codes

```
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48166
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48167
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48168
[review][review_1.json] Error code: undefined, LegacyId: 48169
```

And improve the error message to make it more readable.

#### 2. Data Integration Rules

**2.1 Handling Overlapping Data:**

- All submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Informix data
- Not all Informix data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch
- When overlaps exist, merge the data appropriately:
  - Use ElasticSearch data for newer fields/properties not present in Informix
  - Use Informix data as the authoritative source for core fields
  - Preserve unique identifiers from both systems to maintain backward compatibility

**2.2 ID Mapping:**

- Create a mapping between legacy IDs and new nanoIDs
- Ensure all relationships are properly maintained during the migration
- Preserve legacy IDs in the new schema for backward compatibility

#### 3. Validation Requirements

**3.1 Data Quality Checks:**

- Verify all enum mappings against lookup tables (submission_status_lu, submission_type_lu, etc.)
- Ensure required fields contain valid values
- Validate relationships between submissions and uploads
- Confirm data integrity across all imported records
- Verify that ElasticSearch and Informix data are properly integrated

**3.2 API Response Validation:**

- All Submissions API endpoints must return actual database records
- POST/PUT/PATCH operations must persist to migrated data
- Pagination must work with large datasets
- Filter parameters must produce correct results
- Download endpoints must function correctly with the imported data


### Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the feat/submissions-api branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema if necessary (get approval before making changes)
   - Data migration scripts for both Informix and ElasticSearch data
   - Any necessary modifications to existing services to work with the imported data
   - Tests to verify data integrity and API functionality
   - Error handling mechanisms for migration failures

2. Data Migration Script
   - Provide scripts for migrating both Informix and ElasticSearch data to the new system
   - Use the official [Prisma seeding](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/prisma-migrate/workflows/seeding) workflow for data imports
   - JSON-to-Prisma Transformation Logic:
     - Implement logic to transform both data sources into Prisma-compatible formats
     - Handle data merging when records exist in both sources
   - Batch Import with Progress Tracking:
     - Enable batch data imports with a mechanism to track progress
     - Handle the large volume of data efficiently
   - Rollback Mechanism for Failed Imports:
     - Ensure a rollback mechanism is in place to handle failed imports

3. Validation Suite
   - Postman collection testing all Submissions API endpoints with imported data
   - A document in markdown format with data consistency checks results between legacy/new systems
   - Tests to verify proper integration of data from both sources
   - Tests specifically validating the handling of problematic review records

4. Documentation (in markdown format)
   - Data mapping specification document detailing how fields from both sources map to the new schema
   - Deployment guide
   - Data reconciliation guide explaining how overlaps between data sources were handled

### Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api) as the code base.
- Don't break any existing functionalities of the codebase.
- Ensure Swagger docs are accessible at http://localhost:3000/api-docs.
- The goal is to have all Submissions API endpoints return real data from the migrated legacy system while maintaining backward compatibility.
- Remember that all submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Informix data, but not all Informix data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch.

title: PieceFinder Computer Vision Proof of Concept Challenge
description:
# Challenge Overview

Welcome to the **PieceFinder Computer Vision Proof of Concept Challenge**!  
In this challenge, you will help shape the technical foundation of PieceFinder, an innovative app for puzzle enthusiasts. PieceFinder aims to assist users in finding specific puzzle pieces and counting all pieces using state-of-the-art computer vision and AI.  
Your mission is to research, prototype, and demonstrate core technologies that will power the app’s puzzle-solving features.

---

## Background

A customer is creating an app called **Piece Finder** or **PieceFinder**.  
The app is for puzzle lovers and the main function is to assist puzzlers when they are stuck trying to find a specific piece of a puzzle.  

For many who do physical puzzles, they enjoy the quiet, focused time and satisfaction of completing a challenging puzzle.  
However, it can be extremely frustrating if they spend hours searching for a single piece.  

PieceFinder seeks a way to graciously assist puzzlers through a mobile application that accomplishes a few key things:  

1. When a puzzler wants assistance, they can open the app and either take photos of, or open the camera in the app and scan the area they are stuck, scan the available pieces, and the app guides them to try specific pieces believed to be correct.
2. The app must also be able to **count all puzzle pieces** laid out on a table or desk.  
   This helps verify if the puzzle set is complete (e.g., confirming a 1000-piece puzzle really has 1000 pieces).

> **Note:** Eventually, this technology will be the core of the full PieceFinder app, which will also include user profiles, payments, and tracking completed puzzles.  
> However, for this challenge, focus only on the two technical aspects detailed above.

---

## Core Requirements

Your submission must address the following **four components**:

### 1. Research & Evaluation (Technical Landscape Analysis)
**Goal:** Recommend the most suitable open-source computer vision/AI libraries and tools for mobile-based puzzle piece recognition.

**Tasks:**
- Analyze and compare relevant open-source libraries (e.g., Segment Anything, OpenCV, TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch Mobile, etc.).
- Evaluate available pre-trained models for image segmentation and matching.
- Discuss dataset needs and potential sources for training/validation.
- Provide an architecture overview for implementing visual matching in a cross-platform mobile app.
- Justify your technology choices with clear reasoning, scalability considerations, and mobile deployment feasibility.

---

### 2. Puzzle Piece Image Segmentation
**Goal:** Create a preprocessing pipeline to isolate and extract individual puzzle pieces from a photo or live camera feed.

**Tasks:**
- Use open-source segmentation models (e.g., Segment Anything) to identify and label distinct puzzle pieces, even in cluttered or overlapping scenarios.
- Deliver clean, reusable code with example runs and visualizations of segmented results.

---

### 3. Matching Engine
**Goal:** Prototype an AI-powered system to visually match isolated puzzle pieces to empty slots in a puzzle photo.

**Tasks:**
- Use visual features (shape, color, edge continuity) to recommend the top 3 likely matching pieces.
- Return confidence scores or a visual overlay (color-coded or heatmap) to guide the user.
- Optimize for accuracy and speed.

---

### 4. Feedback & Reinforcement Loop
**Goal:** Design a feedback capture mechanism to improve model accuracy over time using user input.

**Tasks:**
- Allow users to mark suggestions as correct or incorrect.
- Incorporate feedback into the model’s learning pipeline (e.g., retraining approach, ranking adjustments).
- Provide a sample data flow diagram for the feedback loop.
- If a suggestion is incorrect, prompt the user to try other highlighted pieces or rescan.


## Deliverables

- Landscape analysis and technology recommendations (report format, PDF or Markdown).
- Well-documented code for segmentation and matching engines, with example runs.
- Visualizations of segmentation and matching results (images or video clips).
- Feedback loop design and sample data flow documentation.
- **Optional:** Demo app or scripts showcasing the entire flow.


## Judging Criteria (in order of importance)

1. **Accuracy & Speed** — Quality of piece-to-slot matching and recommendation speed.
3. **Robustness** — Performance on complex puzzles with high piece counts and unusual shapes.
4. **Real-world Applicability** — Demonstrates it works in realistic conditions (e.g., low light, messy table).
5. **Scalability** — The solution should scale well as the app grows in popularity.
6. **User Experience** — How simple and intuitive the user experience is (e.g., scanning pieces with minimal manual steps).


## Additional Considerations

- Explore whether a user can scan a puzzle box barcode to retrieve the completed puzzle image online for reference.
- Focus on modular, easily-integratable solutions for future mobile app development.
- Clean code and clear documentation are essential.


## Submission Guidelines

Please submit a **zip file** containing the following items:

1. Source code, reports, and documentation on how to run and review the solution.
2. Documentation explaining how the core requirements were fulfilled.
3. A quick video (hosted on Google Drive, accessible link) demonstrating the solution — include the link in the documentation.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer – Multi-Cloud Infrastructure Automation (Azure + AWS)
description:
We’re looking for an **experienced DevOps Technical Lead** to join a long-term project with one of Topcoder’s enterprise clients. This role demands deep hands-on experience in **Infrastructure as Code, multi-cloud orchestration, GitLab CI/CD, microservice deployment, and observability platforms** across **Azure and AWS**.

You’ll be responsible for building highly automated, secure, and observable DevOps platforms — serving internal teams and real production-grade microservices.

## Responsibilities
* Architect and automate Azure and AWS cloud infrastructure using **Terraform**
* Build and manage **AKS & EKS** clusters, and related services (e.g., APIM, CosmosDB, Azure SQL, Front Door, ADF)
* Create and manage **GitLab CI/CD pipelines** for application builds and deployments using **Helm & Docker**
* Implement **GitOps workflows** using **Argo CD**
* Set up and secure APIs using **OAuth 2.0, SAML,** and **Azure AD**
* Integrate cloud-native and third-party observability tools (e.g., Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Sumo Logic, Azure Monitor, CloudWatch)
* Collaborate with internal engineering teams to define platform requirements and standards
* Evaluate new tools and technologies that can strengthen the platform

## Required Skills
* **8+ years** of experience in DevOps and platform engineering
* Proven expertise in **Terraform** for Azure and AWS resource provisioning
* Strong experience with **AKS, EKS**, and container orchestration
* Hands-on with **GitLab CI/CD, Docker, Helm**
* Deep understanding of **networking, cloud security, and IAM**
* Proficiency with **Argo CD** or similar GitOps tools
* Knowledge of API security protocols: **OAuth 2.0, SAML, AAD**
* Familiarity with observability tools: **Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Sumo Logic, Azure Log Analytics**
* Excellent communication and documentation skills

## Nice to Have
* Azure certifications (e.g., AZ-400, AZ-104) or AWS certifications (e.g., DevOps Engineer Pro)
* Experience with **enterprise-grade CI/CD standards**
* Working knowledge of **cloud cost optimization**

## What You’ll Get
* Competitive pay for the full duration of the project
* Opportunity to work with a globally recognized client
* 100% remote work flexibility
* Supportive Topcoder Gig Work team and community
* Long-term engagement with potential for extension

##  To Apply
Interested candidates must submit the following via the Topcoder Gig Work platform:
✅ Submit **Profile**
📜 Relevant **certificates** (e.g., Azure/AWS/DevOps)
📂 Any other **supporting documents** or portfolios (e.g., GitHub repo links, DevOps architecture samples)

Please ensure your availability overlaps with **EST or PST** work hours.

title: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot - Challenge API Integration Fixes
description:
In [the previous challenge](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/30377431), we implemented the Challenge API integration for the Topcoder Autopilot service to automatically transition challenges through their phases. A code review has identified several issues that need to be fixed to ensure the system works as intended. This First2Finish challenge focuses on addressing these issues to improve the reliability and maintainability of the autopilot service.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- Auth0 for authentication
- REST API integration
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git

### Core Requirements

1. **Implement Auth0 Token Generation**

   The current implementation uses a configurable M2M token which can expire. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement an Auth0 service to generate M2M tokens on the fly
   - Use Auth0 configuration (URL, client ID, client secret, domain, audience) for token generation
   - Remove the static M2M token from the configuration
   - Ensure proper error handling and token caching

2. **Fix Challenge API Pagination**

   The current implementation of **getAllActiveChallenges()** doesn't handle pagination, retrieving only the first 20 records. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement pagination handling to retrieve all active challenges
   - Iterate through all pages until empty results are returned
   - Maintain proper error handling and retry mechanisms
   - Update all services that use this method (recovery service, sync service, etc.)

3. **Fix Challenge Identification**

   The current implementation incorrectly uses projectId for filtering challenges and constructing phase keys. Update the code to:
   
   - Replace **c.projectId === projectId** with proper challengeId filtering
   - Update the phaseKey construction to use **${challenge.id}:${phase.id}** instead of using projectId
   - Ensure consistent use of challengeId throughout the codebase
   - Update any affected methods and services

4. **Remove Unnecessary HTTP Headers**

   The current implementation includes unnecessary headers in API requests. Update the code to:
   
   - Remove the User-Agent header from the **getAuthHeader()** method
   - Ensure only necessary headers are included in API requests
   - Maintain proper authentication headers

5. **Rename Misleading Methods**

   The **getActiveChallenge()** method is poorly named as it doesn't check if the challenge is active. Update the code to:
   
   - Rename the method to a more appropriate name (e.g., **getChallengeById()**)
   - Update all references to this method throughout the codebase
   - Ensure method documentation accurately reflects its functionality

6. **Implement Phase Advancement**

   The **advancePhase()** method is implemented but not used anywhere in the codebase. Update the code to:

   - Call this method when a phase transition is triggered
   - Ensure proper error handling and logging
   - Update the scheduler service to use this method when transitioning phases

7. **Fix Docker Compose Configuration**

   The current Docker Compose configuration command "docker compose up -d" generates a warning about an obsolete **version** attribute. 
   
   ```
   docker-compose.yml: the attribute **version** is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion
   ```
   
   Update the configuration to:
   
   - Remove the obsolete **version** attribute from docker-compose.yml
   - Ensure the Docker Compose file follows the latest best practices
   - Test that the Docker Compose setup works correctly without warnings

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the **feat/challenge-api-integration** branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git, which should include:

1. Complete implementation of all the fixes listed above
2. Comprehensive tests for the new functionality
3. Updated documentation reflecting the changes

title: TopCoder Lookups API- Fix Review Issues
description:
The Lookups API v6 service has been implemented to provide lookup data for countries, devices, and educational institutions. A code review has identified several issues that need to be addressed to ensure the service meets the requirements. This First2Finish challenge focuses on fixing these issues to improve the quality and functionality of the API.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- PostgreSQL
- Prisma ORM
- Swagger/OpenAPI
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/lookups-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/lookups-api-v6)

### Core Requirements

1. **Fix Response Format**

   The API should return responses in the format **{ fromDB: true, result }** but currently returns data directly. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement a consistent response format across all endpoints
   - Wrap all responses in the specified format
   - Ensure proper error handling maintains this format

2. **Remove Unnecessary Response Fields**

   The API is currently returning all fields including **createdAt**, **updatedAt**, and **isDeleted** which should not be exposed. Update the code to:
   
   - Remove these fields from all GET endpoints responses
   - Implement response transformers or DTOs to control the output format
   - Check the v5 API response format at https://api.topcoder.com/v5/lookups/countries for reference

3. **Fix API Base Path**

   The endpoints should be served under the **/v6/lookups** base path. Update the code to:
   
   - Configure the correct base path for all endpoints
   - Update Swagger documentation to reflect the correct API version
   - Ensure all routes are properly configured

4. **Fix Swagger Security Configuration**

   The Swagger security is not mapped correctly, showing all endpoints as secured when some may be accessible without authentication. Update the code to:
   
   - Correctly map security requirements in Swagger documentation
   - Ensure public endpoints are properly documented as such
   - Maintain proper security for protected endpoints

5. **Implement Code Uniqueness Validation**

   The database checks for unique names but not for unique codes. Update the code to:
   
   - Implement uniqueness validation for country codes
   - Add appropriate database constraints in the Prisma schema
   - Add validation in the DTOs for code uniqueness
   - Implement proper error handling for duplicate code attempts

6. **Fix ESLint Configuration**

   The current ESLint configuration has an error with the **globals** package import.

   ```
   Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'globals' imported from /lookups-api-v6/eslint.config.mjs
    Did you mean to import "globals/index.js"?
   ```
   
   Update the code to:
   
   - Fix the ESLint configuration in **eslint.config.mjs**
   - Ensure all required dependencies are properly installed
   - Verify that **npm run lint** executes without errors
   - Maintain code quality standards across the codebase

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of the lookups-api-v6 repository, which should include:

1. Complete implementation of all the fixes listed above
2. Updated documentation reflecting the changes (if needed)

title: Topcoder Member API - Legacy Data Import for Member API Migration
description:
This challenge focuses on migrating historical member data from Topcoder's legacy system to the new Prisma-based schema in the Topcoder Member API. In [the previous challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30377016/?type=develop), we successfully migrated the Member API endpoints into the Review API while maintaining backward compatibility. Now, we need to import the legacy submissions data to ensure these endpoints return real data instead of mock responses.

The data to be imported comes from two sources:
1. Legacy Informix database exports (JSON format)
2. ElasticSearch exports from the current Member API

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6)

### Core Requirements:

#### 1. Data Transformation Rules

**1.1 MemberProfile (Dynamo):**

- Convert legacy member records to the new Prisma schema format
- Map member status to the appropriate enum values
- Preserve all metadata including maxRating, addresses, and timestamps
- Ensure proper relationships between member and related entities (memberFinancial, memberHistoryStats, memberStats, memberSkill and memberTraits)

**1.2 MemberStats (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStats records to the new schema format
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberMaxRating, memberDevelopStats, memberDataScienceStats, memberCopilotStats and memberDesignStats)

**1.3 MemberStats Private (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStats records to the new schema format (model memberStats)
- isPrivate should be true, and should set groupId as well.
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberMaxRating, memberDevelopStats, memberDataScienceStats, memberCopilotStats and memberDesignStats)

**1.4 MemberStatsHistory (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStatsHistory records to the new schema format
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberDevelopHistoryStats and memberDataScienceHistoryStats)

**1.5 MemberStatsHistory Private (Dynamo):**

- Transform MemberStatsHistory records to the new schema format
- isPrivate should be true, and should set groupId as well.
- Ensure proper relationships between MemberStats and related entities (memberDevelopHistoryStats and memberDataScienceHistoryStats)

**1.4 ElasticSearch Data:**

- Import data from ElasticSearch exports (members-2020-01.json, memberskills-2020-01.json and memberstats-2020-01.json)
- Map ElasticSearch document fields to the appropriate Prisma schema fields
- Preserve all metadata and relationships

#### 2. Data Integration Rules

**2.1 Handling Overlapping Data:**

- All members, member skills and member stats in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Dynamo data
- Not all Dynamo data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch
- When overlaps exist, merge the data appropriately:
  - Use ElasticSearch data for newer fields/properties not present in Dynamo
  - Use Dynamo data as the authoritative source for core fields
  - Preserve unique identifiers from both systems to maintain backward compatibility

**2.2 ID Mapping:**

- Create a mapping between legacy IDs and new auto increased ID
- Ensure all relationships are properly maintained during the migration
- Preserve legacy IDs in the new schema for backward compatibility

#### 3. Validation Requirements

**3.1 Data Quality Checks:**

- Ensure required fields contain valid values
- Validate relationships between each entities
- Confirm data integrity across all imported records
- Verify that ElasticSearch and Dynamo data are properly integrated

**3.2 API Response Validation:**

- All Member API endpoints must return actual database records
- POST/PUT/PATCH operations must persist to migrated data
- Pagination must work with large datasets
- Filter parameters must produce correct results
- Download endpoints must function correctly with the imported data


### Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema if necessary (get approval before making changes)
   - Data migration scripts for both Dynamo and ElasticSearch data
   - Any necessary modifications to existing services to work with the imported data
   - Tests to verify data integrity and API functionality
   - Error handling mechanisms for migration failures

2. Data Migration Script
   - Provide scripts for migrating both Dynamo and ElasticSearch data to the new system
   - Use the official [Prisma seeding](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/prisma-migrate/workflows/seeding) workflow for data imports
   - JSON-to-Prisma Transformation Logic:
     - Implement logic to transform both data sources into Prisma-compatible formats
     - Handle data merging when records exist in both sources
   - Batch Import with Progress Tracking:
     - Enable batch data imports with a mechanism to track progress
     - Handle the large volume of data efficiently
   - Rollback Mechanism for Failed Imports:
     - Ensure a rollback mechanism is in place to handle failed imports

3. Validation Suite
   - Postman collection testing all Member API endpoints with imported data
   - A document in markdown format with data consistency checks results between legacy/new systems
   - Tests to verify proper integration of data from both sources
   - Tests specifically validating the handling of problematic review records

4. Documentation (in markdown format)
   - Data mapping specification document detailing how fields from both sources map to the new schema
   - Deployment guide
   - Data reconciliation guide explaining how overlaps between data sources were handled

### Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6) as the code base.
- Don't break any existing functionalities of the codebase.
- The goal is to have all Member API endpoints return real data from the migrated legacy system while maintaining backward compatibility.
- Remember that all submissions in ElasticSearch should have a legacy ID that corresponds to a record in the Dynamo data, but not all Dynamo data will have a corresponding record in ElasticSearch.

title: 72 hours! - Topcoder Review App - Challenge Review Details Page Integration - Part 1

title: Topcoder Resources API: get full role object for resources API
description:
## Project Overview

The current challenge API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the **resources API** to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/77910b35-7ad7-4e67-a953-85d88b7d6cf6) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Data Migration Tool From Dynamo to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/f0cc9d2f-e2c4-48f6-9381-ccfb92330773)

The goal for this F2F challenge is to update response so that the full role object(by roleId) is included in this response.

# Individual Requirements

Currently, the getResources API returns only roleId from the response.
For example, https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/resources?challengeId=00fa4ae0-3ac6-4b9b-b3aa-e2765628e48d&memberId=88774619 will return the following:
```json
[
    {
        "id": "0c51eb77-26e7-4b2a-866d-51db760af375",
        "challengeId": "6360c18d-e5fa-4442-a2e1-bf9982acf3b4",
        "memberId": "16096823",
        "memberHandle": "hohosky",
        "roleId": "cfe12b3f-2a24-4639-9d8b-ec86726f76bd",
        "createdBy": "mess",
        "created": "2025-04-01T22:10:07.000Z",
        "rating": 2427
    }
]
```
We should update response of resources APIs so that the full role object(by roleId) is included in this response.
 To be more specificated, we should update getResources API.
# Resources
- Resource API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/tree/develop
- Migration Tool: Will provide in forum

# Submission
Your submission should include:

A patch file against the develop branch of resource API
A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new functionality

title: Topcoder Review API - GitHub Webhook Integration
description:
In this First2Finish challenge, we need to enhance the existing Review API (review-api-v6) by implementing a new API endpoint that captures webhook events from GitHub. The endpoint will listen for Git events, validate them using GitHub's signature verification mechanism, and store the events in a new database table.

### Technology Stack

- Backend: Node.js with NestJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- Review API (review-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git
- GitHub Webhooks Documentation: https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks

### Core Requirements:

1. **Create a New Database Table**

Create a new table named **gitWebhookLog** with the following fields:
- id, type: nanoId, auto generated
- eventId, type: text, not NULL - use the x-github-delivery header value from webhook request
- event, type: text, not NULL - use the x-github-event header value from webhook request
- eventPayload, type: jsonb, not NULL - use to store full webhook event payload
- createdAt, type: timestamp with tz, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

2. **Implement New API Endpoint**

Create a new API endpoint in TC Review API:
- URL: **POST /webhooks/git**
- Security: Each request will be authorized via GitHub's signature verification mechanism
- If authorization fails, request should be rejected with a 403 error

3. **GitHub Webhook Authentication**

Implement GitHub's signature verification mechanism:
- Use the GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET environment variable to store the secret
- Validate the X-Hub-Signature-256 header from GitHub
- Use timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks

4. **Webhook Processing**

Implement webhook processing logic:
- Capture the webhook event
- Log it to the database (create a record in gitWebhookLog)
- Create a handler service that logs the event to console (placeholder for future implementation)
- Respond with 200 OK to the POST request

5. **Documentation**

Create documentation that explains:
- How to generate a GitHub webhook secret
- How to configure the environment variables
- How to set up a webhook in a GitHub repository
- What events are supported

### Implementation Guidelines

1. Ensure all API calls are properly typed using TypeScript interfaces
2. Implement proper error handling for webhook processing
3. Use the existing PrismaService for database operations
4. Use the existing LoggerService for logging

### Submission Guidelines

1. Prisma migration for the new gitWebhookLog table
2. New webhook with controller, service, and guards
4. Documentation on how to set up and use GitHub webhooks with the API

title: Topcoder Identity Service: Tests Improvement
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to rewrite or improve e2e tests, unit tests.

## Detail Requirements
Based on the revised identity service code repository https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop, we should write unit tests and e2e tests thoroughly for the APIs:
- Write E2E tests for each API to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/e2e` folder. 
- Update/Rewrite Unit tests to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under `test/unit` folder.
- All tests should be targeting database data, not just mock. We have a data migration tool  which can be used to import data for tests.
- Log any bugs found during writing tests.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Patch file of latest commit of develop branch for identity API v6
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

title: Topcoder Review API - Kafka Events Consumer Implementation
description:
In this First2Finish challenge, we need to implement a general Kafka events consumer module in the TC Review API. The module should provide a flexible way to consume Kafka events with configurable topics and handlers, proper logging, and easy integration of new event handlers.

### Technology Stack

- Backend: Node.js with NestJS
- Message Broker: Kafka
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- Review API (review-api-v6): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git

### Core Requirements:

1. **Implement KafkaModule**

   Create a new module in the shared modules directory that:
   - Connects to Kafka brokers using configurable settings
   - Provides a flexible way to register and manage event handlers
   - Integrates with the existing application structure

2. **Implement KafkaConsumerService**

   Create a service that:
   - Manages Kafka connections and subscriptions
   - Routes incoming messages to appropriate handlers
   - Provides proper logging before and after handler execution
   - Implements error handling for failed message processing

3. **Implement KafkaHandlerRegistry**

   Create a registry service that:
   - Stores and retrieves event handlers by topic
   - Provides a simple way to register new handlers
   - Returns a list of all registered topics

4. **Implement Base Event Handler**

   Create an abstract base class that:
   - Defines the common interface for all event handlers
   - Provides logging capabilities
   - Standardizes the handler implementation pattern

5. **Implement AVScan Action Scan Handler**

   Create a sample handler that:
   - Subscribes to the 'avscan.action.scan' topic
   - Logs the received event payload
   - Dumps the payload to console

6. **Docker Setup**

Provide Docker configurations that:

- Set up a local development environment
- Include Kafka services
- Enable hot reloading for development
- Configure appropriate environment variables

### Implementation Guidelines

1. Follow NestJS best practices for module and service implementation
2. Use dependency injection for all services
3. Make the Kafka configuration flexible through environment variables
4. Ensure proper error handling and logging throughout the implementation
5. Implement the module in a way that makes adding new event handlers straightforward
6. Integrate with the existing GlobalProvidersModule for application-wide availability

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the develop branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/review-api-v6.git, which should include:

1. Implementation of the Kafka consumer module
2. Implementation of the AVScan Action Scan handler
3. Updated environment variables sample file
4. Updated package.json with new dependencies
5. Documentation explaining the implementation and how to test it

title: Topcoder Review API - Branch Merge and Code Fixes for Review API
description:
