In this challenge, we're aiming to rewrite or improve e2e tests, unit tests and this is the first part which focuses on **SearchController**, **StatisticsController**.

## Detail Requirements
Based on the revised member service code repository https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6/tree/develop, we should write unit tests and e2e tests thoroughly for **SearchController, StatisticsController APIs and related services SearchService and StatisticsService**:
- Write E2E tests for each APIs to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under **test/e2e** folder. You can refer to [Challenge.api.test.js](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/blob/develop/test/e2e/challenge.api.test.js).
- Update/Rewrite Unit tests for each services to make sure **overall coverage, branch coverage and line coverage all >= 90%**, and put each tests under **test/unit** folder.
- All Unit Tests should be targeting database data, not just mock. You can refer to [ChallengeService.test.js](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/blob/develop/test/unit/ChallengeService.test.js). We have a data migration tool  which can be used to import data for tests.
- Log any bugs found during writing tests.
- Rewrite a clear **README** file on how to set up environment and database, how to migrate data, how to deploy locally, how to verify through postman collections;
- Make sure tests code follow best practices and can pass code lint check.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Member Service:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api
- Revised Member Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api-v6/tree/develop

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Patch file of latest commit of develop branch for member API v6
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

title: MS Teams AI Agent Tab App with MCP Gateway Integration
description:
Design and develop a Microsoft Teams **Tab-based application** embedding an **AI Agent** that reasons using **AWS Bedrock LLMs** and orchestrates tool execution via **Topcoder MCP Gateway**. The application will support SSO, and a responsive tab UI with friendly End User capabilities.

---

## Goals

* Build a production-ready Microsoft Teams **Tabs App** with conversational AI capabilities.
* Integrate **AWS Bedrock** for LLM inference and **Topcoder MCP Gateway** for tool orchestration.
* Allow **Users** to interact with tools through a friendly UI powered by AI reasoning.

---

## Features

### 1. Microsoft Teams Tab App

* Built using **React + Fluent UI**
* Installed in Teams and rendered as a tab
* [SSO via **Azure AD / Entra ID**](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/how-to/authentication/tab-sso-overview)
   * When the user opens the Teams tab, Microsoft Teams injects an authentication token.
   * The frontend sends this token to the backend with each request.
   * The backend validates this token to ensure the user belongs to the same Microsoft 365 tenant / organization.
   * Only validated users from the tenant are allowed to interact with the agent and invoke tools.
* Tenant-aware installation
* [Microsoft Teams Tab App Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/what-are-tabs)

### 2. AI Agent (LLM via AWS Bedrock)

* Uses **LangChain JS** with `@langchain/community/llms/bedrock`
* Supports:
  * Prompt orchestration
  * Conversation memory
  * Streaming responses
  * Retry/fallback for throttling and timeouts
* [AWS Bedrock Overview](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/)
* [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs)
* [LangChain Python MCP Adapter (Optional)](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor)

### 3. MCP Gateway Integration

* Connects securely to **Topcoder MCP Gateway**

  * Provided in forums: sample Python code for how to connect to the MCP tools.
* Agent can:

  * Select tools based on prompt
  * Call tools and return structured responses
* Typed schemas for tools
* Environment-aware (dev/test/prod) - make it configurable
* No secrets in code (all in Key Vault / Secrets Manager)
* [Topcoder MCP Endpoint](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)

### 4. End User UX

* User logs in via SSO
* Main interface provides:

  * Prompt input (chat-like)
  * AI-generated summaries
  * Tool execution results (Adaptive cards/cards/tables)
  * Feedback/status indicators (loading, retry, success/failure)

### 5. Expected User Experience and Interactions

The app should deliver an intuitive and efficient experience for non-technical users to interact with backend tools through AI.

#### a. Login & Role-based Access

* Users land on the tab and are automatically signed in via SSO
* The app identifies the user and displays corresponding UI

#### b. Prompt Interaction

* A text input box lets the user type a request (e.g., "Get current project status")
* Upon submission, the agent parses and responds
* The UI shows AI status/progress indicator during processing, (e.g. Thinking, Calling XX tool, processing yyy .. etc)
*  The user should not be allowed to send new response until AI finishes or a stop button should be used (like ChatGPT)

#### c. Streaming Response

* For longer responses, content streams token-by-token (ChatGPT-like experience)
* UI updates in real-time as the model responds

#### d. Tool Invocation Flow

* The LLM selects and invokes an MCP tool behind the scenes
* UI may show:

  * "Invoking Tool: ToolName"
  * Result section with structured content (table, adaptive cards, or text)
* If parameters are missing, the bot prompts the user for them

#### e. Error Handling & Retry

* If a tool fails or times out, the agent retries or gracefully shows an error message
* Common user-friendly errors: "Tool unavailable", "Missing input", "Try again later"

#### f. History / Memory

* (Optional) Session memory retained per user so they can ask follow-up questions
* Example: "What about last week?"

#### g. Chat History Side Panel

* Users will have access to a side panel showing their chat history
* History will display:

  * Prompt entered
  * AI responses
  * Tool names invoked (if any)
  * Timestamps
* Users can click a past interaction to view the full exchange in the main panel
* The panel should support filters (e.g., Today, This Week) and optional search by keyword or tool name

---

## Technology Stack

* **UI:** React.js + Fluent UI (Teams UI)
* **Auth:** Azure AD SSO
* **Agent:** Python [LangChain MCP Adapter](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor) OR Node.js [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs) + AWS Bedrock
* **Tool Invocation:** Topcoder MCP Gateway ([API](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp))
* **Secrets Management:** Azure Key Vault ([Docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview)) or AWS Secrets Manager ([Docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html))
* **Hosting:** AWS (container or static site + backend)

---

## Deliverables

* Source code for:

  * Teams Tab App UI
  * Agent logic (Bedrock + MCP integration)
* Teams manifest
* Documentation:

  * Architecture & sequence diagrams
  * Security and IAM configuration
  * Deployment instructions
  * User Guide
* Demo video:
  * App installation
  * SSO flow
  * User flow with 2 tool executions

---

## References & Resources

* [Microsoft Teams Tab App Dev](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/what-are-tabs)
* [Bot Framework SDK for Node.js](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/javascript/api/botbuilder)
* [Azure AD Authentication for Teams Apps](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/how-to/authentication/auth-aad-sso)
* [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs)
* [LangChain Python + MCP](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor)
* [AWS Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/)
* [Topcoder MCP Gateway API](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)
* [Azure Key Vault](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview)
* [AWS Secrets Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

* App installs as a tab in Teams and authenticates using Azure AD
* Users can enter prompts and receive streaming AI responses
* At least 2 external MCP tools are integrated and callable via the agent
* Secrets are securely stored and never hardcoded
* Deployment is reproducible manually via documented steps

title: Topcoder Identity Service fixing Roles Endpoints Defects
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookuap roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this f2f challenge, we're going to fix the Roles Endpoints defects in the new code.

## Detail Requirements

Compared with v3 java code, the following are missing for roles endpoints:
1, get roles endpoints, should return specified fields according to selector parameter, see https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/blob/6e352321d2d78fb975f212bc30aee1225f70397a/src/api/user/user.controller.ts#L524 as an example;
2, assign role, and deassign role should call dao.checkRole as in the java code, see https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/bf2428ea6997e115b317eb419c5e2c1c6029cdf5/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/RoleResource.java#L429;
3, hasRole endpoint, the logic should call dao.checkRole;
4, We should return role id from assigning or deassign role endpoints;
5, user.service.ts#addUserToDefaultGroups should be implemented.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:
- A patch file against the develop branch of Revised Identity Service latest commit
- A validation markdown file that covers how you verify the functionalities

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

title: AgentGateway Configuration Cookbook and Demos
description:
[AgentGateway](https://agentgateway.dev/) is a lightweight gateway for MCP (Model Context Protocol), agent-to-agent communication, and secure backend integrations. To accelerate adoption, we need a **configuration cookbook, runnable demos, and reference architectures** that are production-quality, easy to reuse, and verifiable.

The output of this challenge will help developers copy/paste working examples, understand architectural trade-offs, and test AgentGateway in real environments.

## Objectives

* Provide **curated, copy/paste-ready configurations** for common AgentGateway use cases.
* Deliver **runnable demos with validation scripts** showing each scenario works end-to-end.
* Document **authentication and authorization** flows with Auth0, including RBAC.
* Show **operational patterns** for logging, observability, and long-term log retention on AWS EFS.
* Include **reference architecture documents and diagrams** for each major scenario.

## Scope of Work

Participants must implement and document **all** of the following scenarios. Each scenario must have both **Runnable Demos** and **Architecture Documents**.

### 1. MCP backends on a single gateway

**Description**: Demonstrate how AgentGateway can connect multiple MCP backends on the same listener, combining HTTP and HTTPS targets securely.

* **Configs:** **mcp-http.yaml** and **mcp-https.yaml**.
* **Runnable Demo:** Connect to [Topcoder MCP server](https://api.topcoder.com/v6/mcp/mcp) over Http (Streamable Http) and another [Topcoder MCP server](https://api.topcoder.com/v6/mcp/mcp)  and [HuggingFace MCP server](https://hf.co/mcp) over https (with backend TLS config and root CA and Streamable HTTPS).
* MCP connection should be fast, reliable and secure
* **Architecture Documents:**
  * Sequence diagram of request flow.
  * Component diagram with listener, routes, policies.

### 2. Logging and long-term retention
**Description**: Provide operational guidance on persisting AgentGateway logs, ensuring long-term availability using AWS EFS for compliance and troubleshooting.

* **Configs:** EFS mount + logging pipeline.
* **Runnable Demos:**
  * Docker deployment writing logs to EFS.
  * Systemd-based service logging to EFS.
  * Validation script confirming logs in mounted EFS path.
* **Architecture Documents:**
  * Logging pipeline diagram.
  * Operational doc (**LOGGING-EFS.md**) including step by step EFS setup (AWS) and mount instructions.

### 3. MCP authentication and authorization
**Description**: Secure MCP access using Auth0 for authentication and enforce role-based authorization with CEL rules.

* **Auth Modes to Explain & Contrast (in docs):**
  1. **Authorization Server Proxy** – AgentGateway acts as an OAuth2/OIDC proxy in front of the upstream (token issuance/refresh proxied via gateway).
  2. **Resource Server Only** – AgentGateway validates JWTs issued by an external Authorization Server (Auth0), enforcing scopes/roles locally.
  3. **Passthrough** – Gateway forwards Authorization headers without validation; upstream/backends perform auth.

* **Configs (must be runnable):**

  * **mcp-auth0-authn.yaml** – Resource Server Only (Auth0 issuer/JWKS, audience).
  * **mcp-authz-admin-user.yaml** – **mcpAuthorization** CEL policies for Admin vs User.
  * Example snippets for Proxy and Passthrough modes.

* **CEL Rules (Admin vs User):**

  ```
  mcpAuthorization:
    rules:
      # Admins can call Tool A and Tool B (assumes permission tools:invoke)
      - 'jwt["permissions"].exists(p, p == "tools:invoke") && mcp.tool.name in ["toolA", "toolB"]'
      # Users can only call Tool A (assumes permission tools:read)
      - 'jwt["permissions"].exists(p, p == "tools:read") && mcp.tool.name == "toolA"'
  ```

  *Notes:* Adjust to your Auth0 token shape (e.g., **permissions**, **scope**, or custom claims like https://example.com/roles). Provide both **scope-based** and **role-claim** variants in the doc.

* **Runnable Demo (fully working sample is required):**

  1. **Auth0 Setup**: Create an API (set **Identifier/Audience**), define scopes **tools:read**, **tools:invoke**; create two users (Admin with **tools:invoke** + **tools:read**; User with **tools:read**); create an application to obtain tokens (Device Flow or Test tab).
  2. **Start AgentGateway** with **mcp-auth0-authn.yaml** and **mcp-authz-admin-user.yaml**.
  3. **Validate**:
     * **curl** with **Admin token** invoking Tool A and Tool B, expect **200/allowed**.
     * **curl** with **User token** invoking Tool A, expect **200/allowed**; invoking Tool B, expect **403/denied**.
     * Include example curl commands and expected JSON responses/log lines in **MCP-AUTH-AUTHZ.md**.
  4. **Passthrough check**: run a variant where gateway does not validate tokens (**backendAuth.passthrough**) and show upstream enforcement.

* **Architecture Documents:**

  * **Sequence diagram**: Client > AgentGateway (JWT validate via JWKS) > MCP backend > response.
  * **Component diagram**: Auth0 (AS), AgentGateway (Resource Server), Policy Engine (CEL), MCP backends, token stores/JWKS.
  * Detailed guide: **docs/MCP-AUTH-AUTHZ.md** explaining **Authorization Server Proxy vs Resource Server Only vs Passthrough**, token acquisition, claim mapping, and troubleshooting.

### 4. Backend TLS for backend connections
**Description**: Show how AgentGateway secures backend connections with TLS and mutual TLS for enhanced trust and encryption.

* **Configs:** **backend-tls.yaml**.
* **Runnable Demo:**
  * Launch demo HTTPS backend with TLS/mTLS.
  * Validate secure connection via curl.
* **Architecture Documents:**
  * Diagram of cert exchange and validation.
  * Doc (**BACKEND-TLS.md**).

### 5. Backend Authentication

**Description**: Demonstrate various backend authentication strategies supported by AgentGateway, including API keys, JWT passthrough, and AWS SigV4 signing.

* **Configs:** **backend-auth-api-key.yaml, backend-auth-passthrough.yaml, backend-auth-aws.yaml.**
* **Runnable Demo:**
  * Inject API key inline and via file.
  * JWT passthrough.
  * AWS SigV4 signing.
  * Validate each via curl.
* **Architecture Documents:**
  * Diagrams showing each backend auth type.
  * Doc (**BACKEND-AUTH.md**).

### 6. LLM Policies
**Description**: Configure AgentGateway to work with multiple LLM providers while enforcing spending controls to manage costs.

* **Configs:** **llm-multiprovider.yaml** with spending controls.
* **Runnable Demo:**
  * Configure multi-provider (OpenAI + Anthropic).
  * Validate requests with spending controls enforced.
* **Architecture Documents:**
  * Diagram of gateway routing to multiple LLM providers.
  * Doc (**LLM-POLICIES.md**).

## Deliverables & Structure

Submit a repository with the following structure:

```
- `configs/`
  - `mcp/`
    - `mcp-http.yaml` (MCP over HTTP)
    - `mcp-https.yaml` (MCP over HTTPS with backend TLS)
    - `mcp-auth0-authn.yaml` (Resource Server Only with Auth0)
    - `mcp-authz-admin-user.yaml` (CEL rules for Admin vs User)
  - `backend-security/`
    - `backend-tls.yaml` (HTTPS + mTLS examples)
    - `backend-auth-api-key.yaml`
    - `backend-auth-passthrough.yaml`
    - `backend-auth-aws.yaml`
  - llm/
    - llm-multiprovider.yaml

- `scripts/`
  - `docker-compose.yml` (optional playground stack)
  - `validate.sh` (basic validations for running demos)
  - `setup-efs.sh` (automate AWS EFS creation and mount)

- `diagrams/`
  - `auth0-mcp-sequence.png` (or .svg)
  - `topology-overview.png` (or .svg)
  -  `backend-tls-sequence.png` (or .svg)
  - `logging-efs.png` (or .svg)
  - `llm-policies.png` (or .svg)


- `docs/`
  - `README.md` (root quickstart)
  - `LOGGING-EFS.md` (options, steps, and validation)
  - `MCP-AUTH-AUTHZ.md` (Auth0 setup + CEL policies + examples)
  - `BACKEND-TLS.md` (TLS/mTLS examples)
  - `BACKEND-AUTH.md` (API key, passthrough, AWS)
  - `LLM-POLICIES.md` (config, spending control explanation)

- `certs`
(sample certs or scripts to generate)

```

All config files must be runnable with **agentgateway -f <file>** and covered by instructions.

## Technical Requirements and Example Snippets

Below are representative snippets; your repo must include full, runnable files with comments.

### 1) Two MCP backends (HTTP and HTTPS) on one listener

- HTTP (Streamable HTTP):

```
binds:
- port: 3000
  listeners:
  - name: default
    protocol: HTTP
    routes:
    - name: mcp-http
      backends:
      - mcp:
          targets:
          - name: mcp-http
            mcp:
              host: http://localhost:3005/mcp/
```

- HTTPS with backend TLS (self-signed root):

```
binds:
- port: 3000
  listeners:
  - name: default
    protocol: HTTP
    routes:
    - name: mcp-https
      backends:
      - mcp:
          targets:
          - name: mcp-https
            mcp:
              host: https://localhost:3443/mcp/
      policies:
        backendTLS:
          root: ./certs/root-ca.pem
          # Optional mTLS
          # cert: ./certs/client.pem
          # key:  ./certs/client-key.pem
```

### 2) Logging and EFS retention

- Where logs are stored by default: AgentGateway writes structured logs to stdout/stderr (visible in terminal, Docker logs, or journald). There is no built-in file logger; persistence is achieved via the hosting environment.
- Required: implement ONE of the following patterns and document it:
  1. Docker-based
     - Run agentgateway as a container
     - Mount EFS to host path (e.g., **/mnt/efs/agentgateway-logs**)
     - Use a logging sidecar (e.g., **fluent-bit**) or Docker **--log-driver local** with **docker logs** shipped to files on the mounted path
  2. Systemd-based
     - Mount EFS to **/mnt/efs/agentgateway-logs**
     - Use a unit file that pipes stdout to rotating files on EFS (e.g., via ExecStart=/bin/sh -c 'agentgateway -f /etc/agentgateway/config.yaml 2>1 | rotatelogs /mnt/efs/agentgateway-logs/gateway.%Y%m%d.log 86400')
- Provide:
  - EFS creation steps (Terraform or bash script)
  - Mount commands for Amazon Linux/Ubuntu
  - Validation steps to prove logs are written to EFS

### 3) MCP authn/authz with Auth0

- Resource Server Only example using Auth0:

```
mcpAuthentication:
  issuer: https://YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN/
  jwksUrl: https://YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN/.well-known/jwks.json
  resourceMetadata:
    resource: http://localhost:3000/mcp
    scopesSupported:
      - tools:read
      - tools:invoke
    bearerMethodsSupported: [header, body, query]
```

- Role/scope-based authorization (Admin vs User):

```
mcpAuthorization:
  rules:
    # Admins can call everything
    - 'jwt["permissions"].exists(p, p == "tools:invoke") && mcp.tool.name in ["toolA","toolB"]'
    # Users can only call toolA
    - 'jwt["permissions"].exists(p, p == "tools:read") && mcp.tool.name == "toolA"'
```

- Include setup steps:
  - Create API in Auth0, configure audience and assign permissions (scopes)
  - Create two app clients (or use a test app) and two test users: one Admin, one User
  - Show how to obtain tokens (Auth0 Test tab / device flow / client credentials as applicable)
  - Validate with curl + **Authorization: Bearer <token>**
- Include diagrams: request flow + token validation

### 4) Backend TLS (HTTPS + mTLS)

```
policies:
  backendTLS:
    root: ./certs/root-cert.pem
    # mTLS
    cert: ./certs/client.pem
    key:  ./certs/client-key.pem
    # Optionally for testing only
    # insecureHost: true
    # insecure: true
```

- Provide a demo backend (e.g., a small HTTPS echo server with a self-signed CA) and instructions to regenerate certs

### 5) Backend Authentication

- API key:

```
policies:
  backendAuth:
    key: "$MY_API_KEY"
```

- API key from file:

```
policies:
  backendAuth:
    key:
      file: /etc/agentgateway/keys/mykey
```

- Passthrough JWT (keep original Authorization header):

```
policies:
  backendAuth:
    passthrough: {}
```

- AWS signing (SigV4):

```
policies:
  backendAuth:
    aws:
      region: us-west-2
      # Optional explicit creds; omit to use instance/role/default chain
      accessKeyId: "$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"
      secretAccessKey: "$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"
      sessionToken: "$AWS_SESSION_TOKEN"
```

### 6) LLM Policies

Multiple Providers Example

```
binds:
- port: 3000
  listeners:
  - routes:
    - backends:
      - ai:
          groups:
          - providers:
            - name: openai
              provider:
                openAI:
                  model: gpt-3.5-turbo
              backendAuth:
                key: "$OPENAI_API_KEY"
            - name: gemini
              provider:
                gemini:
                  model: gemini-1.5-flash-latest
              backendAuth:
                key: "$GEMINI_API_KEY"
        policies:
          localRateLimit:
            - maxTokens: 10000
              tokensPerFill: 10000
              fillInterval: 1h
              type: tokens
```

## Validation & Demos

For **each scenario**, include a short **Validation Guide** with the following sections:

* **Prerequisites** – list required tools, environment variables, API keys, or accounts.
* **Run Instructions** – the exact **agentgateway -f <config>** command or docker/systemd invocation needed.
* **Test Steps** – curl commands, UI/Playground interactions, or other checks to confirm functionality.
* **Expected Results** – sample responses, status codes, or log outputs that indicate success.
* **Cleanup** – steps to stop services, remove test data, or unmount volumes.

At the repository root, provide a **scripts/validate.sh** that automates a basic smoke test:

* Verifies required environment variables are set (e.g., API keys).
* Executes minimal checks for each scenario (curl an endpoint, grep for expected output).
* Returns a non-zero exit code if any check fails.


## Submission Guidelines

- Zip the repo or provide a Git URL
- Include a root **README.md** that links to each sub-doc and lists tested OS (Linux) and versions used
- Include diagrams in **diagrams/** as **.png** or **.svg**
- Provide all sample certs in **certs/` or include scripts to generate them
- Ensure configs are YAML or JSON supported by AgentGateway (prefer YAML)

## Judging Criteria (100 points)

- Completeness (35): All required scenarios implemented and verifiable
- Quality (25): Clear docs, reproducible steps, secure defaults, error handling
- Technical depth (20): Correct use of AgentGateway features; thoughtful policies
- Operational excellence (10): Logging/EFS robustness; rotation; verification
- Clarity of diagrams (10): Accurate, readable, useful

## Timeline and Checkpoints

- Submission phase: 5-7 days
- Review and appeals per Topcoder standard process

## References

- [Configuration overview](https://agentgateway.dev/docs/configuration/overview/)
- [HTTP listeners](https://agentgateway.dev/docs/configuration/listeners/http/)
- [Tls Listners](https://agentgateway.dev/docs/configuration/listeners/tls/)
- [Backends](https://agentgateway.dev/docs/configuration/backends/)
- [Routes](https://agentgateway.dev/docs/configuration/routes/)

title: AI Aided Asset Versioning Platform User Panel Frontend Challenge
description:
### **Challenge Overview**

Marketing teams spend significant time manually adapting creative assets to different channels (Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.) and formats (Story, Feed, Carousel, etc.). This challenge aims to define the system for a platform that uses AI to automate asset resizing, adaptation, and export.

The system will support two components:
* **Admin Panel** for managing platforms, formats, and AI behavior
* **User Panel** for uploading assets and generating adapted variants

Your task in this challenge is to create the react.js frontend for the user panel and integrate with the provided backend apis. 
### **Tech Stack**
* Python 3
* Postgres
* React.js

---

### **Functional Scope**

Design: https://marvelapp.com/prototype/11a2f99e/screen/97582714
- Signup is not in scope
- Forgot password is not in scope

The backend codebase is provided in the forum, and you are responsible to integrate the user panel with apis too. 
For detailed UI to api mapping, please check the document in the forum. 

#### Login Screen
* User can login with username and password, after login, the user can be redirected to admin or user panel based upon user's role.  (No need to have two separate login screen like design)

#### User Panel

* **Upload Workflow**

  * Upload high-resolution assets (e.g., PNG, JPEG)
  * Validate format and size constraints

* **Asset Adaptation**

  * Select desired platforms and formats
  * Edit mode - Manual edit (user can type the text and select the logo etc.). Note that the manual editing is handled in the frontend, and after applying changes, you need to save the metadata and the updated image to the backend. 
  * Preview variant outputs
  * Trigger batch generation of all selected assets

* **Download & Delivery**

  * Download generated files individually or in bulk (e.g., ZIP)
  * Track status of asset generation jobs

On this page: https://marvelapp.com/prototype/11a2f99e/screen/97686410, please add a Select AI Provider dropdown before the "Generate With AI" button. 

### **Deliverables**
- zip file containing the source code
- readme with deployment guide and verification steps

title: Topcoder Ratings Calculation Service Postgres Schema Design
description:
## Project Overview

The current Ratings Calculation Service works and solves a lot of problems we had with read the data from Informix, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the Ratings Calculation Service to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from Informix.

This challenge focuses on designing the database schema (using Prisma ORM) and get OpenAPI documentation updated for future usage. 
The goal is to ensure the schema and API documentation align with the Ratings Calculation Service.

## Requirements
### 1. Prisma Schema

- Define the Prisma Schema for all tables from the Informix under [src/models](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop/src/models)
Others may also be defined in other forms as Informix and ElasticSearch required.

- Include **createdBy**, **createdAt**, **updatedBy**, **updatedAt** in all tables.

- Add indexes when applicable such as frequently queried fields.

- A file that explain how the transformation from Informix to Prisma schema(Postgres) is done and how to verify.

### 2. OpenAPI Spec

Update the Swagger to reflect the update of schema and the exist API.
- update accordingly based on the transformation schema to Prisma Schema
- remove app-version 
- others which are missing or not consistent with exist controllers

## Technology Stack

- Backend: NodeJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger

## Resources
- Member API code: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop
- Prisma: [https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma)
- Sample Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

## Submission Guidelines

- Prisma schema file.
- updated Swagger file.
- A readme file explaining the transformation from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema and verification.

## Judging Criteria:

- Completeness of all transformations from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema
- Clarity and accuracy of OpenAPI documentation.
- Adherence to Prisma best practices (e.g., indexes, naming).

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Winner will need to submit a patch file against the dev branch of [Member API](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop)

title: Update HuggingFace MCP Integration for Topcoder Intelligence Assistant
description:
Enhance the existing [Topcoder Intelligence Assistant](https://huggingface.co/spaces/Topcoder-Platform/topcoder-intelligence-assistant) HuggingFace Space to properly integrate with MCP APIs, remove mock/fallback data, and fix skill-tag mismatches.

## Requirements

1. **Remove Mock Data & Enforce Live MCP Calls**

   * Eliminate all hardcoded/mock responses.
   * Always call MCP for live data.
   * If MCP/OpenAI fails, show a **clear UI error message only** (no mock/fallback data).

2. **Fix “Your Skills & Technologies” Section**

   * Currently mixes challenge skills with challenge tags.
   * Remove “Your Skills & Technologies” section from UI and backend.
   * Use only **“Current Interests & Goals”** for query search (e.g., “JavaScript”).

3. **UI/UX Error Handling**

   * Display minimal, user-friendly error messages when API calls fail.
   * Ensure no broken or confusing outputs.

## Validation

* Clone repo and run locally to confirm no mock data is used.
* Break MCP/API call intentionally → UI shows error only (no dummy data).
* Query with tech keyword (e.g., “JavaScript” or "AI" or "LLM") → returns expected challenge results via “Current Interests & Goals”.

## Deliverables

1. Updated HuggingFace Space codebase (app.py and related files).
2. Clear README with setup instructions (clone, run, env vars).
3. Deployed and working HuggingFace Space with above fixes.
4. Short demo video (2–3 mins) showing:
   * Live MCP data query
   * Proper error handling
   * Fixed Skills/Technologies section

title: Gamma  Theta Frequency Infusion  Visualization  Scoring UI Challenge
description:
## Overview

In the previous challenge, participants built scripts to **automatically embed gamma (≈40Hz)** and **theta (≈6Hz)** frequencies into existing MP3 songs.

Now, we move to the **UI phase** — your task is to design and implement an **interactive web interface** that allows users to upload or select a song, run the frequency infusion algorithm, and **visually and audibly explore the results**.

This challenge focuses on creating a **functional, intuitive, and visually clear** user interface to showcase both the **audio transformation** and the **analytic proof of infusion**.


---

## Tech Stack

* **Frontend / Backend**: Streamlit (Python 3)
* **Audio Processing**: Use the existing gamma/theta infusion script from the previous challenge
* **Data Visualization**: Matplotlib / Plotly / Streamlit native charts

---

## Functional Requirements

Your UI should:

### 1. Support Two Use Cases

**Use Case 1 – Music Library**

* Provide a **preloaded “Music Library” dropdown or picklist**.
* Each entry should show **Artist – Song Title** (use demo MP3s from the previous challenge).
* Once selected, the user clicks **“Apply Overlay”** to run the algorithm.

**Use Case 2 – Upload Your Own MP3**

* Allow users to upload a `.mp3` file directly.
* After upload, the user can click **“Apply Overlay”** to process it.

---

### 2. Display Results Clearly

Once the overlay process completes, show the following:

#### a. **Audio Players**

* **Input MP3** – playable audio of the original song
* **Output MP3** – playable audio of the infused song
* Also include a **Download button** for the output MP3.

#### b. **Spectrographs Viewer**

* Display **Spectrograph A (Before)** and **Spectrograph B (After)**.
* Present them in a simple **modal viewer or carousel** (user can click “View Overlay Data” → arrow through slides):

  1. **Spectrograph A – Before Processing**
  2. **Spectrograph B – After Processing**
  3. **Metrics Slide** – Gamma Inclusion %, Theta Inclusion %, Gamma 40Hz Adherence, and Processing Time


---

## UI/UX Guidelines

* Focus on **functionality and clarity over aesthetics**.
* Keep the layout simple and demo-friendly — minimal navigation.
* Use a **clean, modern design**, inspired by this reference:
  [https://a4e-bb-demo1.replit.app/](https://a4e-bb-demo1.replit.app/)
* Consistent color palette, readable fonts, and clear labels.
* Each section (Upload/Library, Audio Players, Spectrographs, Metrics, Report) should be distinct and scrollable on one page.

---

## Deliverables

Submit the following as your final package:

1. **Full Source Code**

   * Streamlit app
   * Any helper scripts (processing, scoring, visualization)
   * Sample MP3s for the “Music Library”

2. **README.md** with:

   * Setup instructions (how to run locally)
   * Dependencies
   * Any configuration options (paths, sample data, etc.)
